  1855→
  1856→    async function sendMessage() {
  1857→      const input = document.getElementById('message-input');
  1858→      const text = normalizeText(input.value);
  1859→      if (!text || isLoading) return;
  1860→
  1861→      appendMessage('user', text);
  1862→      input.value = '';
  1863→      
  1864→      // Handle special interactive review commands
  1865→      if (text.toLowerCase().includes('review recommendations') || text.toLowerCase() === 'review') {
  1866→        await showTableBasedReview();
  1867→        return;
  1868→      }
  1869→      
  1870→      // Handle responses during interactive review
  1871→      if (window.waitingForExperienceResponse) {
  1872→        handleExperienceResponse(text);
  1873→        return;
  1874→      }
  1875→      
  1876→      if (window.waitingForSkillsResponse) {
  1877→        handleSkillsResponse(text);
  1878→        return;
  1879→      }
  1880→      
  1881→      // Handle post-analysis question responses
  1882→      if (window.waitingForQuestionResponse) {
  1883→        // Still process the question locally
  1884→        const questionHandled = handleQuestionResponse(text);
  1885→        
  1886→        // Also send to backend to save in history and get LLM response
  1887→        setLoading(true);
  1888→        try {
  1889→          const res = await fetch('/api/message', {
  1890→            method: 'POST',
  1891→            headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
  1892→            body: JSON.stringify({ message: text })
  1893→          });
  1894→          const data = await res.json();
  1895→          
  1896→          if (data.error) {
  1897→            console.error('Backend error saving question response:', data.error);
  1898→          } else if (data.response && !questionHandled) {
  1899→            // Display backend response only if question wasn't handled locally
  1900→            // This allows LLM to acknowledge or provide feedback
  1901→            appendMessage('assistant', data.response);
  1902→          }
  1903→        } catch (error) {
  1904→          console.error('=== QUESTION RESPONSE SAVE ERROR ===');
  1905→          console.error('Error type:', error.name);
  1906→          console.error('Error message:', error.message);
  1907→          console.error('Error stack:', error.stack);
  1908→          console.error('Question text:', text);
  1909→          console.error('Full error:', error);
  1910→          console.error('===================================');
  1911→        }
  1912→        setLoading(false);
  1913→        
  1914→        if (questionHandled) {
  1915→          return;
  1916→        }
  1917→      }
  1918→      
  1919→      // Handle proceed command
  1920→      if (text.toLowerCase() === 'proceed') {
  1921→        if (window.pendingRecommendations) {
  1922→          await showTableBasedReview();
  1923→        } else {
  1924→          // Generate customizations
  1925→          await sendAction('recommend_customizations');
  1926→        }
  1927→        return;
  1928→      }
  1929→      
  1930→      setLoading(true);
  1931→
  1932→      try {
  1933→        const res = await fetch('/api/message', {
  1934→          method: 'POST',
  1935→          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
  1936→          body: JSON.stringify({ message: text })
  1937→        });
  1938→        const data = await res.json();
  1939→        
  1940→        if (data.error) {
  1941→          // Filter out error messages that are just CV data echoes
  1942→          const errorMsg = data.error.toString();
  1943→          if (errorMsg.includes('personal_info') || 
  1944→              errorMsg.includes('experience":') ||
  1945→              errorMsg.includes('"name":') ||
  1946→              errorMsg.match(/^\s*["'{]/)) {
  1947→            console.warn('Backend error was CV data echo, suppressing:', errorMsg);
  1948→          } else {
  1949→            appendMessage('system', 'Error: ' + errorMsg);
  1950→            console.error('Server error:', data.error);
  1951→          }
  1952→        } else if (data.response) {
  1953→          try {
  1954→            // Remove any CV data echoes that the LLM might include
  1955→            let cleanResponse = data.response;
  1956→            
  1957→            // Check if response contains customization JSON (even if mixed with text)
  1958→            const jsonMatch = cleanResponse.match(/\{[\s\S]*?"recommended_experiences"[\s\S]*?\}/);
  1959→            if (jsonMatch) {
  1960→              // Extract just the text part before JSON
  1961→              const textBeforeJson = cleanResponse.substring(0, jsonMatch.index).trim();
  1962→              if (textBeforeJson && textBeforeJson.length > 0 && !textBeforeJson.includes('{"action":')) {
  1963→                // Filter out CV data echoes (lines starting with quotes or containing personal_info)
  1964→                const filteredLines = textBeforeJson.split('\n')
  1965→                  .filter(line => {
  1966→                    const trimmed = line.trim();
  1967→                    return trimmed.length > 0 && 
  1968→                           !trimmed.startsWith('"') && 
  1969→                           !trimmed.includes('personal_info') &&
  1970→                           !trimmed.includes('experience') &&
  1971→                           !trimmed.includes('}: {');
  1972→                  })
  1973→                  .join('\n');
  1974→

    47→---
    48→
    49→## Phase 1 — Test Fixes + Metadata Audit Trail + Startup Config Validation
    50→
    51→**Status**: ✅ Complete | **Tests**: 236/236 passed
    52→
    53→### Changes Made
    54→
    55→| File | Change |
    56→|------|--------|
    57→| `scripts/utils/config.py` | Added `ConfigurationError` exception class. Added `validate_config(provider)` function. Changed `llm_provider` property to return `None` (not `'copilot'`) when no provider is configured — so absence is detectable. |
    58→| `scripts/web_app.py` | Added `validate_config, ConfigurationError` to import. Added `validate_config(provider=args.llm_provider)` call at the top of `create_app()`, before any dependencies are initialised. |
    59→| `scripts/utils/cv_orchestrator.py` | Added `rewrite_audit: Optional[List[Dict]] = None` parameter to `generate_cv()`. Added `'rewrite_audit': rewrite_audit or []` to the `metadata` dict written to `metadata.json`. |
    60→| `scripts/utils/conversation_manager.py` | Passed `rewrite_audit=self.state.get('rewrite_audit') or []` in both `generate_cv()` call sites (lines 562 and 932 in original). |
    61→| `test_pdf_generation.py` | Added `patch` to `unittest.mock` import. Wrapped the test-runner loop in `main()` with `patch('scripts.utils.cv_orchestrator.weasyprint.HTML')`, using a `side_effect` that writes minimal `%PDF-1.4\n%%EOF\n` bytes — avoids Google Fonts CDN timeout. |
    62→| `test_performance.py` | Same WeasyPrint mock pattern, wrapped `benchmarks.run_all_benchmarks()` in `main()`. |
    63→| `tests/test_cv_orchestrator.py` | Added WeasyPrint mock to `TestRenderCvHtmlPdf.setUp()` using `unittest.mock.patch` started/stopped via `self._wp_patcher`. Added `self._wp_patcher.stop()` to `tearDown()`. |
    64→| `tests/test_bibtex_parser.py` | **New file.** 26 smoke tests covering `parse_bibtex_file`, `format_publication` (3 styles), `filter_publications` (type/year/keyword), `get_journal_articles`, `get_software_publications`, empty file, and missing-field resilience. |
    65→
    66→### Design Decisions (Phase 1)
    67→
    68→**D1.1 — `llm_provider` hardcoded fallback removed.**
    69→Previously `Config.llm_provider` returned `'copilot'` as a hardcoded default. Changed to return
    70→`None` if neither env var nor config.yaml provides a value. This is safe because `config.yaml`
    71→already has `default_provider: "copilot-oauth"`, and `validate_config()` now catches truly
    72→unconfigured deployments at startup rather than silently using a non-working provider.
    73→
    74→**D1.2 — `validate_config()` receives resolved CLI value.**
    75→`web_app.py` passes `args.llm_provider` to `validate_config()`. The argparse argument defaults
    76→to `config.llm_provider` (which is `None` if unconfigured), so the combination correctly covers
    77→all three valid sources: CLI flag → env var → config.yaml.
    78→
    79→**D1.3 — WeasyPrint mock writes real PDF magic bytes.**
    80→The mock writes `b'%PDF-1.4\n%%EOF\n'` (not an empty file) because `test_utils.validate_pdf_file()`
    81→checks for the `%PDF-` magic bytes header. An empty mock would cause `validate_pdf_file` to return
    82→`False` and mark tests as failed.
    83→
    84→**D1.4 — `tearDown` stops the patcher before `tmp.cleanup()`.**
    85→Patch stop before temp dir cleanup avoids a race where the patcher's `__exit__` might try to
    86→restore state in a directory that no longer exists (unlikely but defensive).
    87→
    88→**D1.5 — `rewrite_audit` passed from session state, not generated inside `generate_cv()`.**
    89→`rewrite_audit` is built by `ConversationManager.submit_rewrite_decisions()` and stored in
    90→`self.state['rewrite_audit']`. It's passed to `generate_cv()` as a parameter so
    91→`cv_orchestrator.py` stays decoupled from session state.
    92→
    93→### Test Results
    94→
    95→```
    96→tests/test_cv_orchestrator.py        ...  passed (includes 6 render smoke tests)
    97→tests/test_bibtex_parser.py          ...  26 passed
    98→Full suite: 236 passed, 1 warning in 4.09s
    99→```
   100→
```

    49→## Phase 1 — Test Fixes + Metadata Audit Trail + Startup Config Validation
    50→
    51→**Status**: ✅ Complete | **Tests**: 236/236 passed
    52→
    53→### Changes Made
    54→
    55→| File | Change |
    56→|------|--------|
    57→| `scripts/utils/config.py` | Added `ConfigurationError` exception class. Added `validate_config(provider)` function. Changed `llm_provider` property to return `None` (not `'copilot'`) when no provider is configured — so absence is detectable. |
    58→| `scripts/web_app.py` | Added `validate_config, ConfigurationError` to import. Added `validate_config(provider=args.llm_provider)` call at the top of `create_app()`, before any dependencies are initialised. |
    59→| `scripts/utils/cv_orchestrator.py` | Added `rewrite_audit: Optional[List[Dict]] = None` parameter to `generate_cv()`. Added `'rewrite_audit': rewrite_audit or []` to the `metadata` dict written to `metadata.json`. |
    60→| `scripts/utils/conversation_manager.py` | Passed `rewrite_audit=self.state.get('rewrite_audit') or []` in both `generate_cv()` call sites (lines 562 and 932 in original). |
    61→| `test_pdf_generation.py` | Added `patch` to `unittest.mock` import. Wrapped the test-runner loop in `main()` with `patch('scripts.utils.cv_orchestrator.weasyprint.HTML')`, using a `side_effect` that writes minimal `%PDF-1.4\n%%EOF\n` bytes — avoids Google Fonts CDN timeout. |
    62→| `test_performance.py` | Same WeasyPrint mock pattern, wrapped `benchmarks.run_all_benchmarks()` in `main()`. |
    63→| `tests/test_cv_orchestrator.py` | Added WeasyPrint mock to `TestRenderCvHtmlPdf.setUp()` using `unittest.mock.patch` started/stopped via `self._wp_patcher`. Added `self._wp_patcher.stop()` to `tearDown()`. |
    64→| `tests/test_bibtex_parser.py` | **New file.** 26 smoke tests covering `parse_bibtex_file`, `format_publication` (3 styles), `filter_publications` (type/year/keyword), `get_journal_articles`, `get_software_publications`, empty file, and missing-field resilience. |
    65→
    66→### Design Decisions (Phase 1)
    67→
    68→**D1.1 — `llm_provider` hardcoded fallback removed.**
    69→Previously `Config.llm_provider` returned `'copilot'` as a hardcoded default. Changed to return
    70→`None` if neither env var nor config.yaml provides a value. This is safe because `config.yaml`
    71→already has `default_provider: "copilot-oauth"`, and `validate_config()` now catches truly
    72→unconfigured deployments at startup rather than silently using a non-working provider.
    73→
    74→**D1.2 — `validate_config()` receives resolved CLI value.**
    75→`web_app.py` passes `args.llm_provider` to `validate_config()`. The argparse argument defaults
    76→to `config.llm_provider` (which is `None` if unconfigured), so the combination correctly covers
    77→all three valid sources: CLI flag → env var → config.yaml.
    78→
    79→**D1.3 — WeasyPrint mock writes real PDF magic bytes.**
    80→The mock writes `b'%PDF-1.4\n%%EOF\n'` (not an empty file) because `test_utils.validate_pdf_file()`
    81→checks for the `%PDF-` magic bytes header. An empty mock would cause `validate_pdf_file` to return
    82→`False` and mark tests as failed.
    83→
    84→**D1.4 — `tearDown` stops the patcher before `tmp.cleanup()`.**
    85→Patch stop before temp dir cleanup avoids a race where the patcher's `__exit__` might try to
    86→restore state in a directory that no longer exists (unlikely but defensive).
    87→
    88→**D1.5 — `rewrite_audit` passed from session state, not generated inside `generate_cv()`.**
    89→`rewrite_audit` is built by `ConversationManager.submit_rewrite_decisions()` and stored in
    90→`self.state['rewrite_audit']`. It's passed to `generate_cv()` as a parameter so
    91→`cv_orchestrator.py` stays decoupled from session state.

   100→
   101→The 1 warning is a pre-existing `UserWarning` in `llm_client.py` — not introduced by Phase 1.
   102→
   103→---
   104→
   105→## Phase 2 — Workflow Progress Indicator
   106→
   107→**Status**: ✅ Complete | **Tests**: 236/236 passed
   108→
   109→### Changes Made
   110→
   111→| File | Change |
   112→|------|--------|
   113→| `web/index.html` (CSS ~line 49) | Added `.step.completed { cursor: pointer; }`, `.step.completed:hover` hover feedback, `.step.upcoming { background: #f8fafc; color: #cbd5e1; }`, and `#session-conflict-banner` amber banner CSS. |
   114→| `web/index.html` (HTML ~line 712) | Added `#session-conflict-banner` dismissible amber banner element immediately before the workflow bar. |
   115→| `web/index.html` (HTML ~line 718) | Replaced 5-step bar (`Load Job → Analysis → Customizations → Generated → Complete`) with 8-step bar: `📥 Job Input → 🔍 Analysis → ⚙️ Customise → ✏️ Rewrites → 🔤 Spell Check → 📄 Generate → 🎨 Layout → ✅ Finalise`. Spell Check and Layout have static `.upcoming` class (not yet implemented). |
   116→| `web/index.html` (HTML ~line 778) | Added `tab-rewrite` (✏️ Rewrites) between Customizations and CV Editor tabs. |
   117→| `web/index.html` (JS ~line 833) | Added global `fetch` interceptor that calls `showSessionConflictBanner()` on any 409 response. |
   118→| `web/index.html` (JS ~line 4241) | Replaced `updateWorkflowSteps()` with 8-step version mapping backend phase strings to step visual states; added `handleStepClick(step)` for back-navigation; added `showSessionConflictBanner()`. |
   119→| `scripts/web_app.py` | Added `import threading`. Added `_session_lock = threading.Lock()` + `_LOCK_EXEMPT_PATHS` set + `@app.before_request` (`_acquire_session_lock`) + `@app.teardown_request` (`_release_session_lock`) — state-mutating POSTs return 409 if lock is already held. |
   120→
   121→### Design Decisions (Phase 2)
   122→
   123→**D2.1 — Spell Check and Layout steps are `upcoming` (not implemented).**
   124→Steps `step-spell` and `step-layout` carry a static `.upcoming` CSS class in the HTML; `updateWorkflowSteps()` skips them (`const UPCOMING = new Set(['spell', 'layout'])`). They will be activated when Phases 6 and 12 are implemented. Making them visually distinct from the un-started steps makes the planned UX roadmap visible to the user without being interactive noise.
   125→
   126→**D2.2 — Back-navigation preserves all downstream state.**
   127→Per the approved plan decision: `handleStepClick` navigates to the corresponding viewer tab but does NOT clear any session state. The LLM context will include all prior decisions when the user eventually re-generates.
   128→
   129→**D2.3 — Session lock uses `threading.Lock` with non-blocking `acquire`.**
   130→`blocking=False` means a second concurrent POST request immediately returns 409 without waiting. This is the correct behaviour for a UI that presents a dismissible amber banner — the user should see instant feedback, not a silent hang. An alternative (timeout-based acquire) would be more complex and would still leave the user uncertain while waiting.
   131→
   132→**D2.4 — `teardown_request` is safe when `before_request` returns early.**
   133→When `before_request` returns the 409 response (lock NOT acquired), `request.environ['_session_lock_acquired']` is never set, so `teardown_request` correctly skips the release call. No double-release or lock leak is possible.
   134→
   135→**D2.5 — Load-session and copilot-auth endpoints are lock-exempt.**
   136→`/api/load-session` and `/api/save` modify the session on disk but do not mutate in-memory `conversation` state in ways that race with LLM calls. Auth endpoints are entirely independent. Exempting them prevents a session restore from being blocked by an in-flight analysis call.
   137→
   138→**D2.6 — Global `fetch` interceptor for 409 detection.**
   139→Rather than patching each of the 30+ fetch call sites, an IIFE overrides `window.fetch` at script load time. Any `fetch` response with `status === 409` triggers the amber banner. This is minimal and correct because: (a) we always want to show the banner on 409, (b) the interceptor does not swallow the response — it returns it unchanged so callers can still inspect the 409 body if needed.
   140→
   141→### Test Results
   142→
   143→```
   144→Full suite: 236 passed, 1 warning in 4.48s
   145→```
   146→
   147→---
   148→
   149→## Phase 3 — Analysis Display Upgrade
   150→
   151→**Status**: ✅ Complete | **Tests**: 236/236 passed
   152→
   153→### Changes Made
   154→
   155→| File | Change |
   156→|------|--------|
   157→| `web/index.html` (CSS ~line 128) | Added `.analysis-page`, `.analysis-role-card`, `.analysis-section`, `.skill-grid`, `.skill-badge`, `.skill-badge.missing`, `.preferred-list`, `.kw-badges`, `.kw-badge`, `.kw-rank`, `.mismatch-callout`, `.questions-panel`, `.question-item`, `.q-chip`, `.q-input`, `.questions-submit-btn` — all Phase 3 analysis and question panel styles. |
   158→| `web/index.html` (`populateAnalysisTab` ~line 2353) | Replaced flat list layout with 4-section layout: (1) Role & Domain card; (2) Required Skills grid with `.skill-badge.missing` highlights; (3) Preferred / Nice-to-Have list; (4) ATS Keywords with `#rank` superscript badges. Amber mismatch callout shown above the grid when any required skill is absent from `window._masterSkills`. |
   159→| `web/index.html` (`fetchStatus` ~line 4185) | Added `window._masterSkills` cache: on every status poll, `all_skills` from the server is normalized to lowercase and stored for mismatch detection. |
   160→| `web/index.html` (`buildFallbackPostAnalysisQuestions` ~line 2180) | Added `choices: [...]` arrays to all 4 fallback questions. |
   161→| `web/index.html` (`askPostAnalysisQuestions` ~line 2239) | Updated to preserve `choices` field from LLM response; calls `renderQuestionsPanel()` instead of `showNextQuestion()`. |
   162→| `web/index.html` (new `renderQuestionsPanel`, `selectQChip`, `updateQProgress`, `submitAllAnswers` ~line 2289) | New question panel functions: chips + always-visible text area per question; `updateQProgress()` counts answered questions and enables Submit button when all answered; `submitAllAnswers()` persists and removes panel. |
   163→| `scripts/web_app.py` (`_fallback_post_analysis_questions` ~line 156) | Added `choices: [...]` arrays to all 4 fallback questions (mirrors frontend fallback). |
   164→| `scripts/web_app.py` (`_generate_post_analysis_questions` ~line 196) | Updated LLM prompt schema to include `choices: ["Option A", ...]`; updated response parser to extract and validate `choices` list (max 4 items, stripped, truthy). |
   165→
   166→### Design Decisions (Phase 3)
   167→
   168→**D3.1 — Mismatch detection is client-side, fuzzy substring match.**
   169→The frontend compares `required_skills` against `window._masterSkills` using bidirectional `.includes()`: `ms.includes(skill)` OR `skill.includes(ms)`. This catches common aliases (e.g. "Machine Learning" matches "Machine Learning Research"). It is intentionally permissive — false positives (no amber flag when the skill really is absent) are worse than false negatives (amber flag shown when skill is present under a different name). Phase 9 skill canonicalisation will improve precision.
   170→
   171→**D3.2 — Missing skill badges are highlighted red in the Required Skills grid.**
   172→Skill badges with class `.skill-badge.missing` are rendered in red so the user can immediately see which skills are unmatched without reading the callout carefully. The amber callout provides a text list for screen reader accessibility.
   173→
   174→**D3.3 — Questions panel appends to analysis tab, not chat.**
   175→The previous chat-bubble approach forced sequential answers through the conversation input. The new panel renders all questions together in the analysis tab after the 4-section content. This means the user sees analysis and questions in a single scroll, can answer at their own pace, and can change answers before submitting.
   176→
   177→**D3.4 — "Submit Answers" button is disabled until all text areas are non-empty.**
   178→`updateQProgress()` counts filled text areas, not selected chips. This allows the user to type a custom answer directly without clicking a chip, and the Submit button becomes enabled as soon as all fields have content.
   179→
   180→**D3.5 — `choices` are optional; questions without choices render text area only.**
   181→The LLM or fallback may not always provide choices for every question. The `chips` variable is an empty string when `q.choices` is empty, so no chip div is rendered, just the text area. This is robust to partial LLM responses.
   182→
   183→### Test Results
   184→
   185→```
   186→Full suite: 236 passed, 1 warning in 3.64s
   187→```
   188→
   189→---
   190→
   191→## Phase 4 — Rewrite Review UI Polish
   192→
   193→**Status**: ✅ Complete | **Tests**: 236/236 passed
   194→
   195→### Changes Made
   196→
   197→| File | Change |
   198→|------|--------|
   199→| `web/index.html` (CSS ~line 531) | Replaced `.rewrite-text-row` / `.rewrite-before` two-column grid with `.rewrite-inline-diff` container. Added `del.diff-removed` (red strikethrough) and `ins.diff-added` (green) token styles. Kept `.rewrite-after` for edit-mode textarea. |
   200→| `web/index.html` (CSS ~line 537) | Updated `.rewrite-keyword` to `display:inline-flex` with a `.kw-rank` child badge (dark blue pill, white text). |
   201→| `web/index.html` (new `computeWordDiff` ~line 3825) | LCS word-level diff: tokenises both strings by splitting on `/(\s+)/` (preserving whitespace as tokens), builds O(m×n) DP table, backtracks to produce `[{token, type: 'unchanged'|'removed'|'added'}]` array. |
   202→| `web/index.html` (new `renderDiffHtml` ~line 3862) | Shared helper that converts a diff-token array to `<del>`/`<ins>` HTML (calls `escapeHtml` on each token). |
   203→| `web/index.html` (`renderRewriteCard` ~line 3871) | Replaced side-by-side `.rewrite-text-row` with `.rewrite-inline-diff` block containing computed diff. Stores `data-original` attribute for diff regeneration. Added `#${idx+1}` rank badge to each keyword pill. Added hidden `#rw-after-${cardId}` container for edit flow. |
   204→| `web/index.html` (`applyRewriteAction` ~line 3918) | Edit mode: hides diff div, shows `#rw-after` textarea. Accept/reject from edit: restores diff display, hides edit area. |
   205→| `web/index.html` (`saveRewriteEdit` ~line 3962) | After saving an edit, regenerates the inline diff from `diffEl.dataset.original` → edited text and re-shows the diff panel. |
   206→
   207→### Design Decisions (Phase 4)
   208→
   209→**D4.1 — LCS operates on whitespace-split tokens, not characters.**
   210→Character-level diffs produce unreadable noise for CV bullets ("Reduc**ed** the…" → red/green char-by-char). Word-level with whitespace tokens preserved as separate array elements means the rendered diff is the same text flow as the original, just with `<del>`/`<ins>` wrapping changed words. The `split(/(\s+)/)` capturing-group approach keeps all spaces as array entries so the joined HTML has correct spacing without extra logic.
   211→
   212→**D4.2 — `data-original` stores the original for edit-then-save diff regeneration.**
   213→If the user clicks Edit, changes the text, and saves, the inline diff is recomputed against the true original (not the previously-proposed text). This is the correct behaviour: the card always shows what changed from the CV as-was, not a diff of a diff. The attribute is set once at render time in `renderRewriteCard` and never mutated by JS.
   214→
   215→**D4.3 — `#rw-after-${id}` is kept but hidden by default.**
   216→`applyRewriteAction` and `saveRewriteEdit` were already written to expect this element as the locus of edit-mode UI. Rather than rewriting both functions from scratch, the hidden container pattern keeps those functions minimal: they only add `style.display` toggles alongside `diffEl`. This maintains backward compatibility with any future callers that expect `#rw-after-text-${id}` to hold the proposed text.
   217→
   218→**D4.4 — Sticky tally bar and `step-rewrite` wiring were already correct.**
   219→The `.rewrite-tally-bar { position: sticky; top: 0 }` was implemented in an earlier pass. `updateWorkflowSteps()` already maps `'rewrite_review'` → `step-rewrite`. No additional changes were needed for those two Phase 4 acceptance criteria.
   220→
   221→**D4.5 — Keyword pill rank badge uses array position, not a score field.**
   222→The plan says "weight rank badge … position in `keywords_introduced` array". The LLM already returns keywords in priority order. The badge renders `#1`, `#2`, … using `.map((k, idx) => …)`. No server-side change is needed: the ordering is preserved by the existing JSON serialisation.
   223→
   224→### Test Results
   225→
   226→```
   227→Full suite: 236 passed, 1 warning in 3.92s
   228→```
   229→
   230→---
   231→
   232→## Phase 5 — Publications Template Block + Human DOCX
   233→
   234→**Status**: ✅ Complete (commit: see git log)
   235→
   236→### Changes Made
   237→
   238→| File | Change |
   239→|------|--------|
   240→| `scripts/requirements.txt` | Added `docxtpl>=0.20.0` |
   241→| `scripts/utils/llm_client.py` | Added `rank_publications_for_job()` concrete method to `LLMClient` base class — sends full pub list + job_analysis to LLM; returns ranked list with `relevance_score`, `rationale`, `authority_signals`, `venue_warning`, `formatted_citation`; falls back gracefully on any error |
   242→| `scripts/utils/cv_orchestrator.py` | `_select_publications()` now includes `'key'` (cite key) in each returned dict; `_select_content_hybrid()` honours `customizations['accepted_publications']` / `customizations['rejected_publications']` lists when filtering publications; `_prepare_cv_data_for_template()` adds `total_publications_count` to `template_metadata`; `_generate_human_docx()` replaced with full python-docx direct construction (Calibri 11pt, 1-inch margins, section headings with bottom border, conditional Publications + Certifications sections) |
   243→| `scripts/utils/conversation_manager.py` | `_execute_action('generate_cv')` now extracts `publication_accepted` / `publication_rejected` from `post_analysis_answers` and injects them into `customizations` before calling `generate_cv()` |
   244→| `templates/cv-template.html` | Added certifications block in page-1 sidebar; added separate publications page `{% if publications %}`; added CSS for `.pub-list`, `.pub-item`, `.pub-count`, `.pub-venue-warn`, `.cert-name`, `.cert-issuer`; updated hidden ATS plaintext section with CERTIFICATIONS + SELECTED PUBLICATIONS sections |
   245→| `scripts/web_app.py` | Added `/api/publication-recommendations` GET endpoint — returns cached `session.publication_recommendations`; on cache miss calls `llm_client.rank_publications_for_job()`, falling back to `orchestrator._select_publications()`) |
   246→| `web/index.html` | Added `publications-review-section` HTML panel to `populateCustomizationsTabWithReview()`; added `buildPublicationsReviewTable()` async function — fetches `/api/publication-recommendations`, renders DataTable with rank/citation/venue/year/first-author/score/rationale/Accept/Reject columns, defaults all to accepted; added `handlePubAction()` toggle helper; added `submitPublicationDecisions()` — persists accept/reject decisions via `/api/post-analysis-responses`; updated `populateCustomizationsTabWithReview()` to call `buildPublicationsReviewTable()` as third table |
   247→
   248→### Design Decisions (Phase 5)
   249→
   250→**D5.1 — python-docx direct construction instead of docxtpl.**
   251→Creating a valid `.docx` template file with Jinja2 syntax requires carefully hand-crafting Open XML content types, styles, and paragraph runs — error-prone to do programmatically and impossible to preview without Word. The existing python-docx direct-construction approach produces identical output with full test coverage and zero template file dependency. `docxtpl` is added to `requirements.txt` for future use but not used in Phase 5.
   252→
   253→**D5.2 — `_select_publications` now returns dicts with a `key` field.**
   254→The cite key was previously only available as the `dict` key in `self.publications`, not in the returned sub-dicts. Adding it as `'key': key` allows downstream filtering in `_select_content_hybrid` and consistent JSON serialisation in `/api/publication-recommendations`. This is a backward-compatible change (callers only gain a new field).
   255→
   256→**D5.3 — Publication decisions flow through `post_analysis_answers`, not `customizations`.**
   257→The user saves accept/reject decisions via `/api/post-analysis-responses` (reusing the existing clarifying-Q&A endpoint), storing them as `publication_accepted` and `publication_rejected` CSV strings. This keeps the session state shape consistent. `_execute_action('generate_cv')` unpacks these strings into the `customizations` dict immediately before calling `generate_cv()`, so they only affect that one invocation and are not permanently mutated in state.
   258→
   259→**D5.4 — Section is hidden when no publications bib file is present.**
   260→`buildPublicationsReviewTable()` gets `recommendations: []` from the endpoint and calls `section.style.display = 'none'` rather than showing an empty table or an error. Users without a `publications.bib` file see an uncluttered Customisation step.
   261→
   262→**D5.5 — `venue_warning` surfaced as ⚠ tooltip in the DataTable.**
   263→Publications with no `journal` or `booktitle` field in BibTeX get a non-empty `venue_warning` string. The table cell renders this as a `⚠` span with `title` attribute so users can identify and exclude low-quality entries before generating the CV.
   264→
   265→### Test Results
   266→
   267→```
   268→Full suite: 236 passed, 1 warning in 4.53s
   269→```
   270→

   272→
   273→## Phase 6 — Spell/Grammar Check
   274→
   275→**Status**: ✅ Complete (commit: see git log)
   276→
   277→### Changes Made
   278→
   279→| File | Change |
   280→|------|--------|
   281→| `scripts/utils/spell_checker.py` | New module — `SpellChecker` class wrapping `language_tool_python`; lazy JVM init; suppresses `SENTENCE_FRAGMENT`, `PUNCTUATION_PARAGRAPH`, `UPPERCASE_SENTENCE_START` for bullet context; skips all grammar checks for skill context; loads/saves `~/CV/custom_dictionary.json`; `prepopulate_from_skills()` auto-populates from master data |
   282→| `scripts/requirements.txt` | Added `language-tool-python>=3.3.0` |
   283→| `scripts/utils/conversation_manager.py` | `submit_rewrite_decisions()` now advances to `spell_check` phase (was `generation`); new `complete_spell_check(spell_audit)` method — stores `spell_audit[]` in state, advances to `generation`, returns `{flag_count, accepted_count, phase}` |
   284→| `scripts/web_app.py` | Imported `SpellChecker`; added lazy singleton `_spell_checker`; added `GET /api/spell-check-sections` (returns summary + approved-rewrite texts); added `POST /api/spell-check` (single-section check); added `GET /api/custom-dictionary`; added `POST /api/custom-dictionary`; added `POST /api/spell-check-complete` (saves audit, calls `complete_spell_check`) |
   285→| `web/index.html` | Added `tab-spell` tab between Rewrites and CV Editor; removed `step-spell` from UPCOMING set; updated `updateWorkflowSteps()` to handle `spell_check` phase and mark spell step done when phase is `generation`/`refinement`; added `spell_check` to `phaseToStep` map; added `spell` to `stepToTab` map; changed post-rewrite submission to navigate to spell tab instead of calling `generate_cv`; added `populateSpellCheckTab()`, `completeSpellCheckFastPath()`, `renderSpellSuggestions()`, `applySpellReplacement()`, `dismissSpellSuggestion()`, `addSpellWord()`, `submitSpellCheckDecisions()` |
   286→| `tests/test_conversation_manager.py` | Updated `test_phase_advances_to_generation` + `test_empty_decisions_returns_zeros` to expect `'spell_check'`; added `TestCompleteSpellCheck` class with 5 tests |
   287→
   288→### Design Decisions (Phase 6)
   289→
   290→**D6.1 — Lazy JVM init on `SpellChecker._get_tool()`.**
   291→LanguageTool starts a local Java process. Doing this at import/app-init time would add several seconds to startup and fail if Java is unavailable. Lazy init on the first `/api/spell-check` call keeps startup fast and allows graceful degradation.
   292→
   293→**D6.2 — `phase: spell_check` inserted between `rewrite_review` and `generation`.**
   294→The plan calls for a dedicated spell-check workflow step. Rather than running spell check inside the `generate_cv` action (which would block cancellation or re-run), it's a separate phase with its own `complete_spell_check()` transition method. The `generate_cv` action doesn't check the incoming phase, so it still works when called from `spell_check` directly (fast-path).
   295→
   296→**D6.3 — Zero-flag fast-path auto-advances without rendering the panel.**
   297→If all sections return 0 suggestions, `populateSpellCheckTab()` immediately calls `/api/spell-check-complete` with an empty audit, shows a brief success message, refreshes status, and calls `generate_cv`. No user interaction is required.
   298→
   299→**D6.4 — Sections are fetched from session state, not from a pre-generation render.**
   300→The spell check runs on: (a) the professional summary from master data, and (b) the `proposed` text of every approved rewrite. This is the text the user has already seen and approved — checking it prevents typos introduced during edit mode. Post-generation HTML text is not re-checked (that would require parsing the output, added complexity without clear benefit).
   301→
   302→**D6.5 — Custom dictionary pre-populates from skill names.**
   303→Technical skill names (e.g. "scikit-learn", "dplyr", "GWAS") would generate false MORFOLOGIK flags. `_prepopulate_spell_dict()` (called on first `/api/spell-check` request) loads all skills from `master_data` + the candidate's name into the custom dictionary in `~/CV/custom_dictionary.json`.
   304→
   305→### Test Results
   306→
   307→```
   308→Full suite: 241 passed, 1 warning in 4.84s  (+5 new tests)
   309→```
   310→

   312→
   313→## Phase 7 — ATS Validation Report + Page Count
   314→
   315→**Status**: ✅ Complete (commit: `d9f284b`) | **Completed**: 2026-03-11
   316→
   317→### Changes Made
   318→
   319→| File | Change |
   320→|------|--------|
   321→| `scripts/utils/cv_orchestrator.py` | New module-level `validate_ats_report(output_dir, job_analysis)` function (~180 lines) running 16 checks: DOCX text selectable, zero tables, zero shapes, contact in body, standard headings, Heading 1 style, consistent date formats, ATS keyword presence, publications-heading exact match; HTML JSON-LD present/valid/knowsAbout/required-fields; WeasyPrint renders without error; WeasyPrint clipping warnings; PDF US Letter dimensions (612×792 pts via pypdf); returns `(checks, page_count)` |
   322→| `scripts/web_app.py` | Updated import to `from utils.cv_orchestrator import CVOrchestrator, validate_ats_report`; added `GET /api/ats-validate` endpoint — calls `validate_ats_report()`, caches `page_count` in session state, returns `{ok, checks, page_count, summary: {pass, warn, fail}}` |
   323→| `web/index.html` | Overhauled `populateDownloadTab()` (now `async`); fetches `/api/ats-validate`; displays page-count badge (amber if `<1.5` or `>3` pages); renders collapsible per-check table with ✓/⚠/✕ icons; blocks download buttons per failure format (DOCX/HTML/PDF blocked independently; `ats_keyword_presence` fail blocks all formats); updated 2 callers to `await populateDownloadTab()` |
   324→
   325→### Design Decisions (Phase 7)
   326→
   327→**D7.1 — `validate_ats_report()` is a module-level function, not a class method.**
   328→Validation operates on generated file paths, not on `CVOrchestrator` instance state. A module-level function is easier to unit test in isolation and does not require a fully initialised `CVOrchestrator` (which loads master data and LLM client).
   329→
   330→**D7.2 — Format-specific blocking with one cross-format exception.**
   331→DOCX-specific failures block only the DOCX download; HTML/JSON-LD failures block only the HTML download; PDF failures block only the PDF download. The `ats_keyword_presence` check (format='all') blocks all downloads because the content is shared: if keywords are missing from the generated text, they are missing in every output format.
   332→
   333→**D7.3 — Page count via WeasyPrint render, not PDF page count.**
   334→Using `weasyprint.HTML(string=html).render().pages` avoids a second PDF parse pass (pypdf is still used for the US Letter dimension check). The WeasyPrint render is already needed for checks 13 and 15 (render-without-error + clipping warnings), so `page_count` is a free byproduct.
   335→
   336→**D7.4 — Publications heading check requires exactly "Publications" in ATS DOCX.**
   337→ATS parsers target the standard heading label "Publications". The human-readable template uses "Selected Publications (N of M)" — that heading variant must only appear in the human DOCX, not the ATS DOCX. The check fails if the ATS DOCX contains a "publications" heading that is not exactly "Publications".
   338→
   339→**D7.5 — Date-format consistency checks Mon YYYY vs MM/YYYY only.**
   340→The spec says mixed formats within one document are flagged. The em-dash vs en-dash vs hyphen separator is not enforced (too many false positives from different OS clipboard sources). Only structural format mixing (month-name format alongside numeric format) is flagged as fail.
   341→
   342→### Test Results
   343→
   344→```
   345→Full suite: 241 passed, 1 warning in 4.74s  (no new tests needed — validate_ats_report tested via test_cv_orchestrator.py existing tests)
   346→```
   347→
   348→---
   349→
   350→## Phase 8 — Phase Re-entry / Iterative Refinement
   351→
   352→**Status**: ✅ Complete (commit: `bf26797`) | **Completed**: 2026-03-11 | **Tests**: 252/252 passed
   353→
   354→### Changes Made
   355→
   356→| File | Change |
   357→|------|--------|
   358→| `scripts/utils/conversation_manager.py` | Added `_STEP_TO_PHASE` class dict mapping frontend step labels to internal phase strings. Added `back_to_phase(target_phase)` — sets `state['phase']`, `iterating`, `reentry_phase` without clearing any downstream state; saves session. Added `re_run_phase(target_phase)` — builds `_downstream_context()` string (prior approvals, omitted/emphasised experiences/skills, accepted spell-check fixes) and passes as `_prior_context` in `user_prefs`; re-executes LLM call for analysis/customization/rewrite_review; stores prior and new outputs; saves session. |
   359→| `scripts/web_app.py` | Added `POST /api/back-to-phase` endpoint wrapping `conversation.back_to_phase()`. Added `POST /api/re-run-phase` endpoint wrapping `conversation.re_run_phase()`; returns 400 if call returns `ok: False`. Updated `/api/status` to return `iterating` and `reentry_phase` fields. |
   360→| `web/index.html` | Added `backToPhase(step)`, `confirmReRunPhase(step)`, `reRunPhase(step)` JS functions. Added "Iterative Refinement" panel at end of `populateDownloadTab()` with three buttons (Refine Customisations, Refine Rewrites, Re-analyse Job). Overhauled `updateWorkflowSteps()`: sets `el.innerHTML` to inject hover-visible `↻` rerun span inside completed steps, amber "↻ Refining" badge on re-entered step when `status.iterating` is true; injected `<style>` for `.step.completed:hover .step-rerun { opacity:1 }`. |
   361→| `tests/test_conversation_manager.py` | Added `TestBackToPhase` (6 tests) and `TestReRunPhase` (5 tests). Fixed `TestReRunPhase.setUp` to configure `mock_llm.recommend_customizations.return_value` and `mock_llm.analyze_job_description.return_value` as dicts to avoid `MagicMock` JSON-serialisation error in `_save_session`. |
   362→
   363→### Design Decisions (Phase 8)
   364→
   365→**D8.1 — `back_to_phase` accepts both frontend step labels and internal phase strings.**
   366→`_STEP_TO_PHASE.get(target_phase, target_phase)` passes through unrecognised strings unchanged. This means the frontend can pass either `'customizations'` (UI label) or `'customization'` (internal) and both resolve correctly.
   367→
   368→**D8.2 — Downstream state is never cleared by back-navigation.**
   369→Per the approved plan decision. `back_to_phase` only mutates `state['phase']`, `state['iterating']`, and `state['reentry_phase']`. All generated files, approved rewrites, experience/skill decisions, and spell-check audit survive the transition. This lets the LLM improve on the last pass rather than starting fresh.
   370→
   371→**D8.3 — `_downstream_context()` is a nested closure, not a method.**
   372→It reads `self.state` directly via closure. This keeps the context-building tightly coupled to the re-run logic without polluting the class API with a semi-private helper. If it needs testing in isolation later it can be extracted.
   373→
   374→**D8.4 — Re-run sets `phase = 'customization'` for both analysis and customization re-runs.**
   375→After re-running analysis, the user should proceed through customisation again (their prior selections may no longer apply). Setting phase to `'customization'` rather than back to `'job_analysis'` means the frontend lands the user in the right next step without an extra click.
   376→
   377→**D8.5 — ↻ rerun icon is injected into `el.innerHTML`, not a child element.**
   378→`updateWorkflowSteps` previously set only `el.classList`. Switching to `el.innerHTML` allows injecting the `<span class="step-rerun">↻</span>` inside the step element. The span's `onclick` uses `event.stopPropagation()` to prevent the enclosing `handleStepClick` from also firing.
   379→
   380→### Test Results
   381→
   382→```
   383→tests/test_conversation_manager.py  ...  252 passed  (+11 new tests)
   384→Full suite: 252 passed, 1 warning in 5.04s
   385→```

   388→
   389→## Phase 9 — Skills Canonicalisation + Bullet Reordering
   390→
   391→**Status**: ✅ Complete (commit: `6c96ea5`) | **Completed**: 2026-03-11 | **Tests**: 272/272 passed
   392→
   393→### Changes Made
   394→
   395→| File | Change |
   396→|------|--------|
   397→| `scripts/data/synonym_map.json` | New file. 80 ML/data-science/CS term mappings used for bidirectional ATS keyword expansion and alias deduplication (`ML`→`Machine Learning`, `NLP`, `GCP`, `k8s`, `sklearn`, …). |
   398→| `scripts/utils/cv_orchestrator.py` | Added `_load_synonym_map()` + lookup indices (`_synonym_map`, `_canonical_index`, `_expansion_index`) loaded in `__init__`. Added `canonical_skill_name(name)` public method. `_organize_skills_by_category()`: deduplicates skills by canonical name — merges `ML` + `Machine Learning` into one entry, keeps higher-years entry, populates `aliases[]`. `_optimize_skills_for_ats()`: expands both ATS keywords and required_skills via synonym map before scoring so bidirectional abbreviation/full-form matching works. `_select_content_hybrid()`: new per-experience bullet ordering block — auto-sorts `achievements` by token+synonym overlap with `ats_keywords`; applies explicit `customizations['achievement_orders']` (list of original indices) when present; stores result as `exp['ordered_achievements']`. |
   399→| `scripts/utils/conversation_manager.py` | In `generate_cv` action: inject `state.get('achievement_orders', {})` into `customizations` before calling `orchestrator.generate_cv()`. |
   400→| `scripts/web_app.py` | `GET /api/synonym-lookup?term=X` → `{term, canonical, found}`. `GET /api/synonym-map` → full map. `POST /api/reorder-bullets` → accepts `{experience_id, order:[int...]}` and persists in `state['achievement_orders']`; empty `order` resets to relevance-sorted. |
   401→| `templates/cv-template.html` | Skills: render `skill.aliases` list in a `<span class="skill-alias">` with tooltip on both page-2 and page-3 skill columns. Experience bullets: use `exp.ordered_achievements` if defined, else fall back to `exp.achievements`, on both page-2 and page-3 experience loops. |
   402→| `web/index.html` | ↕ button added to each experience row in the customisation table. `showBulletReorder(expId, expTitle)`: fetches achievements for experience, builds modal with numbered list and ↑/↓ row-swap buttons. `moveBullet()`, `_updateBulletArrows()`, `saveBulletOrder()` (calls `/api/reorder-bullets`), `resetBulletOrder()` (passes empty order to reset). |
   403→| `tests/test_cv_orchestrator.py` | Added `TestSynonymMap` (7), `TestOptimizeSkillsWithSynonyms` (4), `TestOrganizeSkillsAlias` (5), `TestBulletOrderInSelectContent` (4) — 20 new tests. |
   404→
   405→### Design Decisions (Phase 9)
   406→
   407→**D9.1 — Synonym map is a flat JSON file, not hard-coded.**
   408→`scripts/data/synonym_map.json` is auto-loaded at orchestrator startup and can be extended without touching Python. A missing file degrades gracefully to `{}`.
   409→
   410→**D9.2 — Synonym matching is bidirectional via a single expansion index.**
   411→Both directions (alias→canonical and canonical→alias) are added to `_expansion_index`. ATS keywords are expanded before scoring, so a skill named `ML` matches keyword `Machine Learning` and vice versa. Terminology never changes — only matching.
   412→
   413→**D9.3 — Alias deduplication merges entries but preserves original names as aliases.**
   414→When `ML` and `Machine Learning` both appear in master data, the merge produces a single entry named `Machine Learning` (canonical) with `aliases: ['ML']`. The original name is not lost and appears in the CV template's tooltip.
   415→
   416→**D9.4 — Auto bullet sorting defaults to keyword-token overlap.**
   417→No LLM call is needed. A closure counts how many tokens (after synonym expansion) in each bullet text overlap with `ats_keywords`. This is fast, deterministic, and auditable. User-explicit ordering always takes precedence.
   418→
   419→**D9.5 — User bullet ordering is stored outside `customizations`.**
   420→`state['achievement_orders']` is persisted alongside spell-check and rewrite audit data. It is injected into `customizations` just before `generate_cv()` so the orchestrator receives it cleanly without polluting the LLM-facing recommendation structure.
   421→
   422→### Test Results
   423→
   424→```
   425→tests/test_cv_orchestrator.py  ...  272 passed  (+20 new tests)
   426→Full suite: 272 passed, 1 warning in 4.33s
   427→```
   428→
   429→---
   430→
   431→## Phases 10–15 — Planned
   432→
   433→See the approved plan in `.claude/plans/virtual-wibbling-metcalfe.md` for full
   434→specifications of Phases 10–15. Design decisions and implementation notes will be
   435→added here as each phase is implemented.
   436→

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./scripts/utils/scoring.py`

```text
     1→"""
     2→Relevance scoring utilities for content selection.
     3→"""
     4→
     5→import re
     6→from typing import Dict, List, Set, Tuple, Any
     7→from collections import Counter
     8→
     9→
    10→def calculate_relevance_score(
    11→    item: Dict,
    12→    job_keywords: Set[str],
    13→    job_requirements: List[str],
    14→    domain_context: str = ''
    15→) -> float:
    16→    """
    17→    Calculate relevance score for a CV item (experience, skill, achievement).
    18→    
    19→    Scoring factors:
    20→    - Keyword matches in description/text
    21→    - Importance rating (1-10 from master data)
    22→    - Domain relevance matches
    23→    - Audience tags matching job context
    24→    
    25→    Args:
    26→        item: CV data item (experience, skill, achievement)
    27→        job_keywords: Set of keywords from job description
    28→        job_requirements: List of requirement phrases
    29→        domain_context: Domain/industry context from job
    30→        
    31→    Returns:
    32→        Relevance score (0.0 to 100.0)
    33→    """
    34→    score = 0.0
    35→    
    36→    # Base importance from master data (0-40 points)
    37→    importance = item.get('importance', 5)
    38→    score += importance * 4.0
    39→    
    40→    # Keyword matching (0-30 points)
    41→    item_text = _extract_text(item).lower()
    42→    item_keywords = set(_extract_keywords(item_text))
    43→    
    44→    keyword_matches = len(job_keywords.intersection(item_keywords))
    45→    max_keywords = max(len(job_keywords), 1)
    46→    keyword_score = (keyword_matches / max_keywords) * 30.0
    47→    score += keyword_score
    48→    
    49→    # Domain relevance (0-15 points)
    50→    domain_relevance = item.get('domain_relevance', [])
    51→    if isinstance(domain_relevance, list):
    52→        if domain_context and any(domain_context.lower() in dr.lower() for dr in domain_relevance):
    53→            score += 15.0
    54→        elif domain_relevance:  # Has some domain tags
    55→            score += 7.5
    56→    
    57→    # Audience tag match (0-10 points)
    58→    audience = item.get('audience', [])
    59→    if isinstance(audience, list) and audience:
    60→        # Boost if it has audience tags (indicates targeted content)
    61→        score += 10.0
    62→    
    63→    # Requirement phrase matching (0-5 points)
    64→    requirement_matches = sum(
    65→        1 for req in job_requirements
    66→        if req.lower() in item_text
    67→    )
    68→    if requirement_matches > 0:
    69→        score += min(requirement_matches * 2.5, 5.0)
    70→    
    71→    return min(score, 100.0)
    72→
    73→
    74→def _extract_text(item: Dict) -> str:
    75→    """Extract searchable text from an item."""
    76→    text_parts = []
    77→    
    78→    # Common fields
    79→    for field in ['title', 'company', 'description', 'name', 'text', 'summary']:
    80→        if field in item:
    81→            value = item[field]
    82→            if isinstance(value, str):
    83→                text_parts.append(value)
    84→    
    85→    # Achievements/projects
    86→    if 'achievements' in item:
    87→        for ach in item['achievements']:
    88→            if isinstance(ach, dict):
    89→                text_parts.append(ach.get('description', ''))
    90→            elif isinstance(ach, str):
    91→                text_parts.append(ach)
    92→    
    93→    if 'projects' in item:
    94→        for proj in item['projects']:
    95→            if isinstance(proj, dict):
    96→                text_parts.append(proj.get('description', ''))
    97→    
    98→    # Keywords
    99→    if 'keywords' in item:
   100→        keywords = item['keywords']
```
</details></tool-use>

## Exploration Report: Phase 10 Design Patterns

Based on my thorough exploration of the cv-builder codebase, here's a comprehensive analysis of existing patterns for Phase 10 (Persuasion checks + loading state):

---

### 1. Current Validation Patterns

#### Rewrite Constraint Validation
**File**: `/Users/warnes/src/cv-builder/scripts/utils/llm_client.py` (lines 98-146)

The `apply_rewrite_constraints()` static method provides a reference implementation:

```python
@staticmethod
def apply_rewrite_constraints(original: str, proposed: str) -> bool:
    """Return ``True`` if *proposed* is an acceptable rewrite of *original*.
    
    Returns ``False`` when the proposed text removes any number, date, or 
    company/proper name present in the original.
    """
```

**Constraint checks implemented**:
- Numeric tokens (lines 131-134): Validates all numbers, metrics, percentages preserved
- Proper-name tokens (lines 136-144): Ensures Title-Case words (not in stop-word list) are preserved
- Stop-word list built-in (lines 117-128): Excludes common English words and action verbs

**Usage pattern**: Called during rewrite proposal filtering (line 416-424):
```python
for item in raw:
    if self.apply_rewrite_constraints(
        item.get('original', ''), item.get('proposed', '')
    ):
        valid.append(item)
    else:
        warnings.warn(f"propose_rewrites: constraint violation filtered")
```

#### Content Validation Functions
**File**: `/Users/warnes/src/cv-builder/scripts/utils/cv_orchestrator.py`

The `apply_approved_rewrites()` method (lines 641-799) contains validation guards:
- Line 683: `if not LLMClient.apply_rewrite_constraints(original, proposed):` — skips invalid rewrites
- Lines 699-725: Safe bullet parsing with fallback logging for malformed achievement structures
- Lines 764-778: Skill rename validation with category/subcategory checks

#### Recommendation Validation Structure
**File**: `/Users/warnes/src/cv-builder/scripts/utils/llm_client.py` (lines 631-642)

Recommendation objects enforce a 3-part structure:
```python
{
  "recommendation": "Emphasize|Include|De-emphasize|Omit",
  "confidence": "Very High|High|Medium|Low|Very Low",
  "reasoning": "Detailed explanation of why..."
}
```

LLM prompt specification (lines 576-613) documents confidence mapping:
- "Very High": Overwhelming evidence, no contradicting evidence
- "High": Strong evidence clearly supports
- "Medium": Balanced evidence, legitimate trade-offs
- "Low": Limited evidence or significant uncertainty
- "Very Low": Minimal evidence or contradicting evidence

---

### 2. Loading State Implementations

#### CSS & Animation Patterns
**File**: `/Users/warnes/src/cv-builder/web/index.html` (lines 429-464)

**Loading spinner** (line 430-442):
```css
.loading-spinner {
  width: 20px; height: 20px;
  border: 3px solid #f3f3f3;
  border-top: 3px solid #3498db;
  animation: spin 1s linear infinite;
}
@keyframes spin { 0% { transform: rotate(0deg); } 100% { transform: rotate(360deg); } }
```

**Progress bar** (line 444-448):
```css
#loading-progress-bar {
  position: fixed; top: 0; left: 0; height: 3px; background: #6366f1;
  z-index: 9998; transition: width 0.3s ease; width: 0;
  box-shadow: 0 0 8px rgba(99,102,241,0.6);
}
```

**Step pulse animation** (line 449-453):
```css
@keyframes step-pulse {
  0%, 100% { box-shadow: 0 0 0 0 rgba(99,102,241,0.4); }
  50%       { box-shadow: 0 0 0 4px rgba(99,102,241,0.1); }
}
.step.active.loading-step { animation: step-pulse 1.4s ease-in-out infinite; }
```

**Message loading indicator** (line 118-120):
```css
.loading .content { background: #eff6ff; color: #2563eb; border: 1px solid #bfdbfe; }
.loading .content::after { content: ''; animation: dots 1.5s steps(5, end) infinite; }
@keyframes dots { 0%, 20% { content: ''; } 40% { content: '.'; } ... }
```

#### JavaScript Loading State Management
**File**: `/Users/warnes/src/cv-builder/web/index.html` (lines 4838-4865)

The `setLoading(loading, label)` function:
1. **Global flag**: Sets `isLoading = loading` (disables all buttons)
2. **Progress bar animation**: 
   - Creates/shows bar, animates to 70% when loading starts
   - Sets to 100% and removes after 400ms delay when complete
3. **Step pulse**: Toggles `.loading-step` class on active workflow steps
4. **Optional label**: Sets `bar.title` for hover tooltip

```javascript
function setLoading(loading, label) {
  isLoading = loading;
  const buttons = document.querySelectorAll('button');
  buttons.forEach(btn => btn.disabled = loading);
  
  let bar = document.getElementById('loading-progress-bar');
  if (loading) {
    requestAnimationFrame(() => { bar.style.width = '70%'; });
    if (label) bar.title = label;
  } else {
    bar.style.width = '100%';
    setTimeout(() => bar.remove(), 400);
  }
  document.querySelectorAll('.step.active').forEach(el => {
    el.classList.toggle('loading-step', loading);
  });
}
```

#### Async/Fetch Patterns with Loading Feedback
**File**: `/Users/warnes/src/cv-builder/web/index.html` (lines 1856-2024)

The `sendMessage()` pattern (lines 1930-2000+):
```javascript
setLoading(true);
try {
  const res = await fetch('/api/message', {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    body: JSON.stringify({ message: text })
  });
  const data = await res.json();
  
  if (data.error) {
    appendMessage('system', 'Error: ' + errorMsg);
  } else if (data.response) {
    appendMessage('assistant', data.response);
  }
} catch (error) {
  appendMessage('system', `⚠️ Cannot reach server (${error.message})`);
} finally {
  setLoading(false);
}
```

---

### 3. LLM Result Handling

#### Recommendation Object Structure
**File**: `/Users/warnes/src/cv-builder/scripts/utils/conversation_manager.py` (lines 239-293)

Full spec showing all 3 required components:
```python
{
  "id": "exp001",
  "recommendation": "Emphasize",
  "confidence": "Very High",
  "reasoning": "This experience directly matches required role level and domain. Evidence: X, Y, Z. Confidence is very high because..."
}
```

#### Response Parsing & Error Handling
**File**: `/Users/warnes/src/cv-builder/scripts/utils/llm_client.py` (lines 148-168)

The `_parse_json_response()` method:
- Direct `json.loads()` attempt
- Fallback extraction from `\`\`\`json` code blocks
- Fallback extraction from bare `\`\`\`...` blocks
- Raises `ValueError` with error context if all fail

**Usage in proposals** (lines 409-430):
```python
try:
    response = self.chat(messages, temperature=0.3, max_tokens=4096)
    raw = self._parse_json_response(response)
    valid: List[Dict] = []
    for item in raw:
        if self.apply_rewrite_constraints(...):
            valid.append(item)
        else:
            warnings.warn(f"constraint violation filtered (id={item.get('id')})")
    return valid
except Exception as exc:
    warnings.warn(f"propose_rewrites: failed to produce proposals: {exc}")
    return []
```

### 4. Rewrite Data Structure

#### Complete Rewrite Proposal Schema
**File**: `/Users/warnes/src/cv-builder/scripts/utils/llm_client.py` (lines 377-395)

```python
{
  "id":                  "<unique label, e.g. 'summary', 'bullet_exp001_0', 'skill_3'>",
  "type":                "summary" | "bullet" | "skill_rename" | "skill_add",
  "location":            "<path, e.g. 'summary', 'exp_001.achievements[2]', 'skills.core[1]'>",
  "original":            "<exact original text>",
  "proposed":            "<proposed replacement text>",
  "keywords_introduced": ["<kw1>", "<kw2>"],
  "evidence":            "<comma-separated exp IDs, skill_add only>",
  "evidence_strength":   "strong" | "weak",
  "rationale":           "<one sentence explaining the ATS improvement>"
}
```

#### Rewrite Audit Structure
**File**: `/Users/warnes/src/cv-builder/scripts/utils/conversation_manager.py` (lines 624-628)

Built by `submit_rewrite_decisions()`:
```python
audit.append({
    **proposal,           # All fields from original proposal
    'outcome': outcome,   # 'accept' | 'reject' | 'edit'
    'final': final,       # User-edited text (if outcome == 'edit'), else None
})
```

#### Approved Rewrites Structure
**File**: `/Users/warnes/src/cv-builder/scripts/utils/conversation_manager.py` (lines 630-634)

```python
approved_entry = dict(proposal)
if outcome == 'edit' and final is not None:
    approved_entry['proposed'] = final  # Replace with user edit
approved.append(approved_entry)
```

**Metadata preserved in `approved_rewrites`**: `id`, `type`, `location`, `original`, `proposed`, `keywords_introduced`, `evidence_strength`, `rationale`

---

### 5. Error Handling Patterns

#### Backend Error Responses
**File**: `/Users/warnes/src/cv-builder/scripts/web_app.py`

Common pattern (lines 1436-1461 for `/api/rewrites`):
```python
try:
    job_analysis = conversation.state.get('job_analysis')
    if not job_analysis:
        return jsonify({"error": "Job analysis not available..."}), 400
    
    rewrites = orchestrator.propose_rewrites(content, job_analysis)
    conversation.state['pending_rewrites'] = rewrites
    conversation._save_session()
    return jsonify({"ok": True, "rewrites": rewrites, "phase": phase})
    
except Exception as e:
    import traceback
    traceback.print_exc()
    return jsonify({"error": str(e)}), 500
```

#### Frontend Error Handling
**File**: `/Users/warnes/src/cv-builder/web/index.html` (lines 1940-2024)

Multi-layer error detection:
1. **HTTP error check**: `if (!res.ok)` with response body parsing
2. **JSON response error field**: `if (data.error)` appends system message
3. **Catch network errors**: `catch (error)` with fallback messages
4. **Data validation errors**: Type/field checks with logging to console

Example from line 1940-1951:
```javascript
if (data.error) {
  const errorMsg = data.error.toString();
  if (errorMsg.includes('personal_info') || errorMsg.includes('"name":')) {
    console.warn('CV data echo, suppressing...');
  } else {
    appendMessage('system', 'Error: ' + errorMsg);
    console.error('Server error:', data.error);
  }
}
```

---

```python
{
  'phase': 'init|job_analysis|customization|rewrite_review|spell_check|generation|refinement',
  'pending_rewrites': None,        # List[Dict] from propose_rewrites
  'approved_rewrites': [],         # List[Dict] user-accepted or user-edited
  'rewrite_audit': [],             # Full record: proposal + outcome
  # ... other fields (job_analysis, customizations, etc.)
}
```

#### State Transitions

- **Line 1449**: `rewrite_review` → `generation` or skip if no rewrites
- **Line 638**: After `submit_rewrite_decisions()` → `spell_check` phase

_**Agent (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-03-11 16:04:36Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Excellent exploration! Now I have a very comprehensive understanding of existing patterns. Let me synthesize this into a Phase 10 plan. 

Phase 10 needs to:

**Part 1: Persuasion Checks**
From the user story (user-story-persuasion-expert.md), I need to implement checks for:
- US-P1: Narrative Arc — identity alignment, no hedging language
- US-P2: Social Proof — keep metrics, named institutions, authority signals
- US-P3: Loss-Aversion — CAR (Challenge-Action-Result) detection
- US-P4: Rhetorical Quality — strong opening verbs, word count limits, passive voice, no inputs-only bullets
- US-P5 & US-P6: Will be deferred to later phases (cover letter, consistency across documents)

But looking at the code, I see that `apply_rewrite_constraints()` already handles metric preservation. So I need to **extend** this function and add new validation functions for the other checks.

The acceptance criteria from the user story are:
- Every proposed bullet begins with a strong action verb
- Flags bullets >30 words
- Flags passive voice constructions
- Flags bullets with no result clause  
- Rejects any proposal that removes/vagues a numeric metric (already done!)
- Named orgs in first 15 words
- Publication recommendations ranked by job-relevance (already done in Phase 5!)
- CAR structure detection for experience bullets
- Checks summary against generic filler

**Part 2: Loading State**
The UX expert story (user-story-ux-expert.md) says:
- US-U3 (Analysis Results): If analysis takes >3 seconds, show label + estimated duration
- US-U6 (Generation & Output): Multi-step generation with step-by-step progress + checkmarks
- US-U9 (Layout Review): Processing indicator within 300 ms for layout instruction submissions


- `setLoading()` is functional and animates the progress bar to 70% on start, then 100% on completion, but lacks granular step-by-step feedback during generation. The analysis endpoints don't currently track timing or provide intermediate progress updates. I need to instrument `/api/analyze` to emit progress messages, add step reporting to the generation pipeline, and wire the frontend to display these updates as they arrive.

For the persuasion validation layer, I'm adding several check functions to `llm_client.py`: `check_strong_action_verb()` to validate against approved verbs, `check_passive_voice()` to catch "was X-ed" patterns, `check_word_count()` to flag bullets exceeding 30 words, `check_has_result_clause()` to verify outcome language, `check_named_institution_position()` to ensure brands appear in the first 15 words, `check_car_structure()` to detect the Challenge-Action-Result framework, `check_for_hedging_language()` to identify weak phrases like "helped to" or "was involved in", and `check_summary_generic_phrases()` to filter filler language. I'll also extend `conversation_manager.py` with a new persuasion check method to orchestrate these validations.

The API layer needs a `POST /api/persuasion-check` endpoint that processes proposed or approved rewrites, runs the full validation suite, and returns structured warnings with severity levels and recommendations. The frontend gets a "Persuasion Review" panel displaying flagged issues in a table format with options to dismiss or edit, plus a button to proceed once reviewed.

For loading states, I'm modifying `/api/analyze` and `/api/recommend` to include timeout warnings or elapsed time tracking via `/api/status`, and instrumenting `/api/generate` to emit step-by-step progress events like rendering status updates.

Since generation currently runs synchronously in a single `/api/generate` call, the practical solution is having that endpoint track progress internally and return a progress data structure, while `/api/status` exposes a `generation_progress` field during active generation. The frontend can then poll `/api/status` at higher frequency to display step-by-step updates as the PDF renders and converts.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-03-11 16:05:01Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```md
# Phase 10: Persuasion Checks + Loading State

## Context
Phase 10 implements two critical UX improvements aligned with the Persuasion Expert and UX Expert user stories:

1. **Persuasion Checks** — Validate proposed rewrites and generated content against Cialdini/Adams persuasion principles (US-P1–P4): strong action verbs, clean metrics, no hedging language, CAR (Challenge-Action-Result) detection, narrative consistency, etc.

2. **Loading State** — Provide step-by-step progress feedback during long operations (analysis >3s, generation with HTML→PDF pipeline) so users understand what is happening rather than seeing an indeterminate spinner.

---

## Phase 10 Goals
- **Acceptance Criteria (Persuasion)**: Every proposed bullet begins with strong action verb; system flags bullets >30 words, passive voice, results-less bullets; metrics never removed; named institutions in first 15 words; summary free of generic filler.
- **Acceptance Criteria (Loading)**: Analysis/generation progress labelled with step names and checkmarks; all loaders appear within 300 ms; estimated time shown if operation >3 s.
- **Tests**: Maintain 272+ passing tests; add new unit tests for persuasion checks.
- **UI/Code Quality**: Use `/codeReview`, `/cvUiReview` before commit; commit with EDT timestamp + elapsed duration.

---

## Design Decisions

### D10.1 — Persuasion Checks Run Post-Rewrite-Proposal, Pre-Rewrite-Review
Persuasion validation is a **warning system**, not a blocker. After the LLM proposes rewrites, we run persuasion checks and surface findings in the UI (new "Persuasion Review" panel or sidebar badge). Users can acknowledge warnings and proceed, or iterate. This is cheaper than re-running the LLM.

### D10.2 — Eight Persuasion Check Functions in `llm_client.py`
Extend `scripts/utils/llm_client.py` with static methods (like `apply_rewrite_constraints`):
- `check_strong_action_verb(text)` — Rewrite opening word must be in approved list
- `check_passive_voice(text)` — Flag "was X", "be X-ed", "responsible for", "helped to"
- `check_word_count(text)` — Flag >30 words (per US-P4 spec)
- `check_has_result_clause(text)` — Check for outcome language (numbers, "reduced", "increased", "enabled", etc.)
- `check_hedging_language(text)` — Flag "helped to", "was involved in", "assisted with"
- `check_named_institution_position(text, named_orgs)` — Verify branded names in first 15 words
- `check_car_structure(text)` — Detect Challenge-Action-Result pattern (has problem/context before action)
- `check_summary_generic_phrases(text)` — Flag summary against filler list

Each returns `{pass: bool, flag_type: str, severity: 'warn'|'info', details: str}`.

### D10.3 — Persuasion Validation Orchestrated in `conversation_manager.py`
New method `run_persuasion_checks(rewrites, job_analysis, cv_data)` → `List[{location, flag_type, severity, details, proposed_text}]`. Runs all 8 checks on each rewrite, stores results in `state['persuasion_warnings']` alongside `pending_rewrites`.

### D10.4 — New `/api/persuasion-check` POST Endpoint
Accepts `{rewrites: [...]}`, returns `{ok, warnings: [{...}], summary: {total, by_type: {verb: N, passive: N, ...}}}`. Frontend calls this after `/api/rewrites` response.

### D10.5 — Persuasion Review Panel in UI
After rewrite proposals are fetched, check for `persuasion_warnings` via `/api/status`. If warnings exist, show a new **"Persuasion Review"** collapsible panel above the rewrite-review cards:
- Horizontal bar chart: count by flag type
- Expandable warning rows: location, severity, flag_type, proposed text snippet
- "Review All" / "Dismiss All" actions
- Only allows clicking "Submit Rewrite Decisions" after warnings acknowledged (client-side enforcement)

### D10.6 — Generation Progress via Status Polling
During `/api/generate`, the backend tracks step completion and stores in session state:
\```python
state['generation_progress'] = [
  {'step': 'rendering_html', 'status': 'in_progress' | 'complete', 'elapsed_ms': N},
  {'step': 'generating_docx_ats', 'status': 'in_progress' | 'complete', 'elapsed_ms': N},
  ...
]
\```

Frontend polls `/api/status` every 500 ms during generation and renders a step-by-step list with checkmarks.

### D10.7 — Analysis Progress Feedback
`/api/status` returns `analysis_elapsed_ms: N` alongside phase/status. Frontend shows loading label "Analysing job description…" if elapsed >3 s, and estimated time ("~15 seconds remaining") if >5 s base on Phase 1 telemetry.

### D10.8 — All Loading UI Components Appear Within 300 ms
Use a 300 ms timer in JS to ensure spinners, progress bars, step lists all render before the browser's perceived "lag threshold". Avoid flicker by always reserving fixed-height space for async content.

---

## Implementation Tasks

### Task 10.1: Persuasion Check Functions in `llm_client.py` (≈2 h)
1. Add `STRONG_ACTION_VERBS` constant (list of 30+ approved verbs)
2. Add `GENERIC_FILLER_PHRASES` constant for summary checks
3. Implement 8 check functions (15–20 lines each); each returns flag dict
4. Add unit tests: `TestPersuasionChecks` class, 40+ test cases (mix of pass/fail)

**Key file**: `scripts/utils/llm_client.py`

**Files to modify**:
- Add ~120 lines new code (check functions + constants)
- Test coverage: `tests/test_llm_client.py` (new test class)

### Task 10.2: Persuasion Orchestration in `conversation_manager.py` (≈1 h)
1. New method `run_persuasion_checks(rewrites, job_analysis, cv_data)` → iterates rewrites, calls all 8 checks, aggregates results
2. Store in `state['persuasion_warnings']` during rewrite proposal flow
3. Update `/api/rewrites` response to include warning count

**Key file**: `scripts/utils/conversation_manager.py`

**Files to modify**:
- Add ~80 lines (orchestration method + state handling)
- `scripts/web_app.py`: Update `/api/rewrites` endpoint to call orchestration method

### Task 10.3: Backend Progress Tracking (≈1 h)
1. Instrument `scripts/utils/cv_orchestrator.py` `generate_cv()` to track and store step progress:
   - Before HTML render: `state['generation_progress'] = [{'step': 'rendering_html', 'status': 'in_progress'}]`
   - After: mark complete, add elapsed_ms
   - Repeat for DOCX, PDF generation
2. Return progress data structure from `/api/generate` response
3. Update `/api/status` to expose `generation_progress` and `analysis_elapsed_ms`

**Key files**: `scripts/web_app.py`, `scripts/utils/cv_orchestrator.py`

### Task 10.4: Frontend Persuasion Review Panel (≈1.5 h)
1. New `renderPersuasionReviewPanel(warnings)` function in `web/index.html`
   - Horizontal bar chart (canvas or SVG) of warning counts by type
   - Expandable warning rows with edit links to rewrite cards
   - Actions: "Review All", "Dismiss All", "Keep Reviewing" button
2. Integrate into `handleRewritesResponse()` — check for warnings before showing rewrite cards
3. Add CSS for warning panel (styles: severity colors, expandable rows)

**Key file**: `web/index.html`

### Task 10.5: Frontend Generation & Analysis Progress Display (≈1 h)
1. New `displayGenerationProgress(progressList)` function — renders step-by-step checklist:
   - Step name + status indicator (spinner/checkmark)
   - Elapsed time per step
   - Smooth fade-in/out as steps complete
2. Modify `populateDownloadTab()` to poll `/api/status` every 500 ms during generation
3. Update analysis tab to show "Analysing…" message if elapsed >3 s
4. Fine-tune CSS for 300 ms appearance time

**Key file**: `web/index.html`

### Task 10.6: Unit Tests (≈1.5 h)
1. Add `TestPersuasionChecks` to `tests/test_llm_client.py` (40+ cases covering all 8 functions)
   - Verb checks: approved, hedge language, passive voice
   - Position checks: brands in first 15 words, misplaced
   - Structure: CAR detection, result-less bullets
   - Summary: generic phrases detected
2. Add `TestPersuasionOrchestration` to `tests/test_conversation_manager.py` (10+ cases)
3. Integration test: `/api/persuasion-check` endpoint returns correct warnings
4. Target: 300+ total passing tests

---

## Files to Modify

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `scripts/utils/llm_client.py` | +8 check functions, 2 constants, ~120 lines |
| `scripts/utils/conversation_manager.py` | +1 orchestration method, state handling, ~80 lines |
| `scripts/web_app.py` | +/api/persuasion-check endpoint, update /api/rewrites, update /api/status for progress |
| `scripts/utils/cv_orchestrator.py` | Instrument `generate_cv()` to track step progress (~30 lines) |
| `web/index.html` | +Persuasion panel rendering, generation/analysis progress display, CSS, ~200 lines JS |
| `tests/test_llm_client.py` | +TestPersuasionChecks class (40+ test cases) |
| `tests/test_conversation_manager.py` | +TestPersuasionOrchestration class (10+ test cases) |

---

## Test Verification

1. **Unit tests**: Run `python run_tests.py --categories unit` — expect ≥300 passing
2. **Integration**: Start web app, trigger rewrite flow, verify:
   - `/api/persuasion-check` returns correct warnings
   - Generation step progress displayed in tab with checkmarks
   - Analysis shows "Analysing…" if >3 s
3. **UI**: Run `/cvUiReview` to confirm new panels meet UX criteria
4. **Code quality**: Run `/codeReview` to check for regressions

---

## Risk Factors

1. **Persuasion check false positives** — Generic verb/passive-voice detection may flag correct language. Mitigation: Keep STRONG_ACTION_VERBS list comprehensive; run tests against corpus of real CV bullets.

2. **Progress tracking overhead** — Storing state['generation_progress'] per step may add memory; JSON serialisation if session is saved. Mitigation: Clear progress data after generation completes; use lightweight dicts.

3. **UI clutter** — New persuasion panel + progress checklist may overwhelm user. Mitigation: Persuasion panel collapsible by default (warn-count badge visible); progress checklist minimal styling.

---

## Next Phase Dependencies

Phase 11 (Finalise & Archive) depends on Phase 10:
- Session state now includes `persuasion_warnings` and `generation_progress` — must handle in serialisation
- Rewrite decisions may include user acknowledgment of persuasion warnings — track in audit

Phase 12 (Natural-language layout instructions) benefits from:
- Generation progress telemetry to estimate instruction re-render time
- Persuasion checks can validate layout instruction outputs (result clause check may flag stripped context)

```
