   104→
   105→## Phase 2 — Workflow Progress Indicator
   106→
   107→**Status**: ✅ Complete | **Tests**: 236/236 passed
   108→
   109→### Changes Made
   110→
   111→| File | Change |
   112→|------|--------|
   113→| `web/index.html` (CSS ~line 49) | Added `.step.completed { cursor: pointer; }`, `.step.completed:hover` hover feedback, `.step.upcoming { background: #f8fafc; color: #cbd5e1; }`, and `#session-conflict-banner` amber banner CSS. |
   114→| `web/index.html` (HTML ~line 712) | Added `#session-conflict-banner` dismissible amber banner element immediately before the workflow bar. |
   115→| `web/index.html` (HTML ~line 718) | Replaced 5-step bar (`Load Job → Analysis → Customizations → Generated → Complete`) with 8-step bar: `📥 Job Input → 🔍 Analysis → ⚙️ Customise → ✏️ Rewrites → 🔤 Spell Check → 📄 Generate → 🎨 Layout → ✅ Finalise`. Spell Check and Layout have static `.upcoming` class (not yet implemented). |
   116→| `web/index.html` (HTML ~line 778) | Added `tab-rewrite` (✏️ Rewrites) between Customizations and CV Editor tabs. |
   117→| `web/index.html` (JS ~line 833) | Added global `fetch` interceptor that calls `showSessionConflictBanner()` on any 409 response. |
   118→| `web/index.html` (JS ~line 4241) | Replaced `updateWorkflowSteps()` with 8-step version mapping backend phase strings to step visual states; added `handleStepClick(step)` for back-navigation; added `showSessionConflictBanner()`. |
   119→| `scripts/web_app.py` | Added `import threading`. Added `_session_lock = threading.Lock()` + `_LOCK_EXEMPT_PATHS` set + `@app.before_request` (`_acquire_session_lock`) + `@app.teardown_request` (`_release_session_lock`) — state-mutating POSTs return 409 if lock is already held. |
   120→
   121→### Design Decisions (Phase 2)
   122→
   123→**D2.1 — Spell Check and Layout steps are `upcoming` (not implemented).**
   124→Steps `step-spell` and `step-layout` carry a static `.upcoming` CSS class in the HTML; `updateWorkflowSteps()` skips them (`const UPCOMING = new Set(['spell', 'layout'])`). They will be activated when Phases 6 and 12 are implemented. Making them visually distinct from the un-started steps makes the planned UX roadmap visible to the user without being interactive noise.
   125→
   126→**D2.2 — Back-navigation preserves all downstream state.**
   127→Per the approved plan decision: `handleStepClick` navigates to the corresponding viewer tab but does NOT clear any session state. The LLM context will include all prior decisions when the user eventually re-generates.
   128→
   129→**D2.3 — Session lock uses `threading.Lock` with non-blocking `acquire`.**
   130→`blocking=False` means a second concurrent POST request immediately returns 409 without waiting. This is the correct behaviour for a UI that presents a dismissible amber banner — the user should see instant feedback, not a silent hang. An alternative (timeout-based acquire) would be more complex and would still leave the user uncertain while waiting.
   131→
   132→**D2.4 — `teardown_request` is safe when `before_request` returns early.**
   133→When `before_request` returns the 409 response (lock NOT acquired), `request.environ['_session_lock_acquired']` is never set, so `teardown_request` correctly skips the release call. No double-release or lock leak is possible.
   134→
   135→**D2.5 — Load-session and copilot-auth endpoints are lock-exempt.**
   136→`/api/load-session` and `/api/save` modify the session on disk but do not mutate in-memory `conversation` state in ways that race with LLM calls. Auth endpoints are entirely independent. Exempting them prevents a session restore from being blocked by an in-flight analysis call.
   137→
   138→**D2.6 — Global `fetch` interceptor for 409 detection.**
   139→Rather than patching each of the 30+ fetch call sites, an IIFE overrides `window.fetch` at script load time. Any `fetch` response with `status === 409` triggers the amber banner. This is minimal and correct because: (a) we always want to show the banner on 409, (b) the interceptor does not swallow the response — it returns it unchanged so callers can still inspect the 409 body if needed.
   140→
   141→### Test Results
   142→
   143→```
   144→Full suite: 236 passed, 1 warning in 4.48s
   145→```
   146→
   147→---
   148→
   149→## Phase 3 — Analysis Display Upgrade
   150→
   151→**Status**: ✅ Complete | **Tests**: 236/236 passed
   152→
   153→### Changes Made
   154→
   155→| File | Change |
   156→|------|--------|
   157→| `web/index.html` (CSS ~line 128) | Added `.analysis-page`, `.analysis-role-card`, `.analysis-section`, `.skill-grid`, `.skill-badge`, `.skill-badge.missing`, `.preferred-list`, `.kw-badges`, `.kw-badge`, `.kw-rank`, `.mismatch-callout`, `.questions-panel`, `.question-item`, `.q-chip`, `.q-input`, `.questions-submit-btn` — all Phase 3 analysis and question panel styles. |
   158→| `web/index.html` (`populateAnalysisTab` ~line 2353) | Replaced flat list layout with 4-section layout: (1) Role & Domain card; (2) Required Skills grid with `.skill-badge.missing` highlights; (3) Preferred / Nice-to-Have list; (4) ATS Keywords with `#rank` superscript badges. Amber mismatch callout shown above the grid when any required skill is absent from `window._masterSkills`. |
   159→| `web/index.html` (`fetchStatus` ~line 4185) | Added `window._masterSkills` cache: on every status poll, `all_skills` from the server is normalized to lowercase and stored for mismatch detection. |
   160→| `web/index.html` (`buildFallbackPostAnalysisQuestions` ~line 2180) | Added `choices: [...]` arrays to all 4 fallback questions. |
   161→| `web/index.html` (`askPostAnalysisQuestions` ~line 2239) | Updated to preserve `choices` field from LLM response; calls `renderQuestionsPanel()` instead of `showNextQuestion()`. |
   162→| `web/index.html` (new `renderQuestionsPanel`, `selectQChip`, `updateQProgress`, `submitAllAnswers` ~line 2289) | New question panel functions: chips + always-visible text area per question; `updateQProgress()` counts answered questions and enables Submit button when all answered; `submitAllAnswers()` persists and removes panel. |
   163→| `scripts/web_app.py` (`_fallback_post_analysis_questions` ~line 156) | Added `choices: [...]` arrays to all 4 fallback questions (mirrors frontend fallback). |
   164→| `scripts/web_app.py` (`_generate_post_analysis_questions` ~line 196) | Updated LLM prompt schema to include `choices: ["Option A", ...]`; updated response parser to extract and validate `choices` list (max 4 items, stripped, truthy). |
   165→
   166→### Design Decisions (Phase 3)
   167→
   168→**D3.1 — Mismatch detection is client-side, fuzzy substring match.**
   169→The frontend compares `required_skills` against `window._masterSkills` using bidirectional `.includes()`: `ms.includes(skill)` OR `skill.includes(ms)`. This catches common aliases (e.g. "Machine Learning" matches "Machine Learning Research"). It is intentionally permissive — false positives (no amber flag when the skill really is absent) are worse than false negatives (amber flag shown when skill is present under a different name). Phase 9 skill canonicalisation will improve precision.
   170→
   171→**D3.2 — Missing skill badges are highlighted red in the Required Skills grid.**
   172→Skill badges with class `.skill-badge.missing` are rendered in red so the user can immediately see which skills are unmatched without reading the callout carefully. The amber callout provides a text list for screen reader accessibility.
   173→
   174→**D3.3 — Questions panel appends to analysis tab, not chat.**
   175→The previous chat-bubble approach forced sequential answers through the conversation input. The new panel renders all questions together in the analysis tab after the 4-section content. This means the user sees analysis and questions in a single scroll, can answer at their own pace, and can change answers before submitting.
   176→
   177→**D3.4 — "Submit Answers" button is disabled until all text areas are non-empty.**
   178→`updateQProgress()` counts filled text areas, not selected chips. This allows the user to type a custom answer directly without clicking a chip, and the Submit button becomes enabled as soon as all fields have content.
   179→
   180→**D3.5 — `choices` are optional; questions without choices render text area only.**
   181→The LLM or fallback may not always provide choices for every question. The `chips` variable is an empty string when `q.choices` is empty, so no chip div is rendered, just the text area. This is robust to partial LLM responses.
   182→
   183→### Test Results
   184→
   185→```
   186→Full suite: 236 passed, 1 warning in 3.64s
   187→```
   188→
   189→---
   190→
   191→## Phase 4 — Rewrite Review UI Polish
   192→
   193→**Status**: ✅ Complete | **Tests**: 236/236 passed
   194→
   195→### Changes Made
   196→
   197→| File | Change |
   198→|------|--------|
   199→| `web/index.html` (CSS ~line 531) | Replaced `.rewrite-text-row` / `.rewrite-before` two-column grid with `.rewrite-inline-diff` container. Added `del.diff-removed` (red strikethrough) and `ins.diff-added` (green) token styles. Kept `.rewrite-after` for edit-mode textarea. |
   200→| `web/index.html` (CSS ~line 537) | Updated `.rewrite-keyword` to `display:inline-flex` with a `.kw-rank` child badge (dark blue pill, white text). |
   201→| `web/index.html` (new `computeWordDiff` ~line 3825) | LCS word-level diff: tokenises both strings by splitting on `/(\s+)/` (preserving whitespace as tokens), builds O(m×n) DP table, backtracks to produce `[{token, type: 'unchanged'|'removed'|'added'}]` array. |
   202→| `web/index.html` (new `renderDiffHtml` ~line 3862) | Shared helper that converts a diff-token array to `<del>`/`<ins>` HTML (calls `escapeHtml` on each token). |
   203→| `web/index.html` (`renderRewriteCard` ~line 3871) | Replaced side-by-side `.rewrite-text-row` with `.rewrite-inline-diff` block containing computed diff. Stores `data-original` attribute for diff regeneration. Added `#${idx+1}` rank badge to each keyword pill. Added hidden `#rw-after-${cardId}` container for edit flow. |
   204→| `web/index.html` (`applyRewriteAction` ~line 3918) | Edit mode: hides diff div, shows `#rw-after` textarea. Accept/reject from edit: restores diff display, hides edit area. |
   205→| `web/index.html` (`saveRewriteEdit` ~line 3962) | After saving an edit, regenerates the inline diff from `diffEl.dataset.original` → edited text and re-shows the diff panel. |
   206→
   207→### Design Decisions (Phase 4)
   208→
   209→**D4.1 — LCS operates on whitespace-split tokens, not characters.**
   210→Character-level diffs produce unreadable noise for CV bullets ("Reduc**ed** the…" → red/green char-by-char). Word-level with whitespace tokens preserved as separate array elements means the rendered diff is the same text flow as the original, just with `<del>`/`<ins>` wrapping changed words. The `split(/(\s+)/)` capturing-group approach keeps all spaces as array entries so the joined HTML has correct spacing without extra logic.
   211→
   212→**D4.2 — `data-original` stores the original for edit-then-save diff regeneration.**
   213→If the user clicks Edit, changes the text, and saves, the inline diff is recomputed against the true original (not the previously-proposed text). This is the correct behaviour: the card always shows what changed from the CV as-was, not a diff of a diff. The attribute is set once at render time in `renderRewriteCard` and never mutated by JS.
   214→
   215→**D4.3 — `#rw-after-${id}` is kept but hidden by default.**
   216→`applyRewriteAction` and `saveRewriteEdit` were already written to expect this element as the locus of edit-mode UI. Rather than rewriting both functions from scratch, the hidden container pattern keeps those functions minimal: they only add `style.display` toggles alongside `diffEl`. This maintains backward compatibility with any future callers that expect `#rw-after-text-${id}` to hold the proposed text.
   217→
   218→**D4.4 — Sticky tally bar and `step-rewrite` wiring were already correct.**
   219→The `.rewrite-tally-bar { position: sticky; top: 0 }` was implemented in an earlier pass. `updateWorkflowSteps()` already maps `'rewrite_review'` → `step-rewrite`. No additional changes were needed for those two Phase 4 acceptance criteria.
   220→
   221→**D4.5 — Keyword pill rank badge uses array position, not a score field.**
   222→The plan says "weight rank badge … position in `keywords_introduced` array". The LLM already returns keywords in priority order. The badge renders `#1`, `#2`, … using `.map((k, idx) => …)`. No server-side change is needed: the ordering is preserved by the existing JSON serialisation.
   223→
   224→### Test Results
   225→
   226→```
   227→Full suite: 236 passed, 1 warning in 3.92s
   228→```
   229→
   230→---
   231→
   232→## Phase 5 — Publications Template Block + Human DOCX
   233→
   234→**Status**: ✅ Complete (commit: see git log)
   235→
   236→### Changes Made
   237→
   238→| File | Change |
   239→|------|--------|
   240→| `scripts/requirements.txt` | Added `docxtpl>=0.20.0` |
   241→| `scripts/utils/llm_client.py` | Added `rank_publications_for_job()` concrete method to `LLMClient` base class — sends full pub list + job_analysis to LLM; returns ranked list with `relevance_score`, `rationale`, `authority_signals`, `venue_warning`, `formatted_citation`; falls back gracefully on any error |
   242→| `scripts/utils/cv_orchestrator.py` | `_select_publications()` now includes `'key'` (cite key) in each returned dict; `_select_content_hybrid()` honours `customizations['accepted_publications']` / `customizations['rejected_publications']` lists when filtering publications; `_prepare_cv_data_for_template()` adds `total_publications_count` to `template_metadata`; `_generate_human_docx()` replaced with full python-docx direct construction (Calibri 11pt, 1-inch margins, section headings with bottom border, conditional Publications + Certifications sections) |
   243→| `scripts/utils/conversation_manager.py` | `_execute_action('generate_cv')` now extracts `publication_accepted` / `publication_rejected` from `post_analysis_answers` and injects them into `customizations` before calling `generate_cv()` |
   244→| `templates/cv-template.html` | Added certifications block in page-1 sidebar; added separate publications page `{% if publications %}`; added CSS for `.pub-list`, `.pub-item`, `.pub-count`, `.pub-venue-warn`, `.cert-name`, `.cert-issuer`; updated hidden ATS plaintext section with CERTIFICATIONS + SELECTED PUBLICATIONS sections |
   245→| `scripts/web_app.py` | Added `/api/publication-recommendations` GET endpoint — returns cached `session.publication_recommendations`; on cache miss calls `llm_client.rank_publications_for_job()`, falling back to `orchestrator._select_publications()`) |
   246→| `web/index.html` | Added `publications-review-section` HTML panel to `populateCustomizationsTabWithReview()`; added `buildPublicationsReviewTable()` async function — fetches `/api/publication-recommendations`, renders DataTable with rank/citation/venue/year/first-author/score/rationale/Accept/Reject columns, defaults all to accepted; added `handlePubAction()` toggle helper; added `submitPublicationDecisions()` — persists accept/reject decisions via `/api/post-analysis-responses`; updated `populateCustomizationsTabWithReview()` to call `buildPublicationsReviewTable()` as third table |
   247→
   248→### Design Decisions (Phase 5)
   249→
   250→**D5.1 — python-docx direct construction instead of docxtpl.**
   251→Creating a valid `.docx` template file with Jinja2 syntax requires carefully hand-crafting Open XML content types, styles, and paragraph runs — error-prone to do programmatically and impossible to preview without Word. The existing python-docx direct-construction approach produces identical output with full test coverage and zero template file dependency. `docxtpl` is added to `requirements.txt` for future use but not used in Phase 5.
   252→
   253→**D5.2 — `_select_publications` now returns dicts with a `key` field.**
   254→The cite key was previously only available as the `dict` key in `self.publications`, not in the returned sub-dicts. Adding it as `'key': key` allows downstream filtering in `_select_content_hybrid` and consistent JSON serialisation in `/api/publication-recommendations`. This is a backward-compatible change (callers only gain a new field).
   255→
   256→**D5.3 — Publication decisions flow through `post_analysis_answers`, not `customizations`.**
   257→The user saves accept/reject decisions via `/api/post-analysis-responses` (reusing the existing clarifying-Q&A endpoint), storing them as `publication_accepted` and `publication_rejected` CSV strings. This keeps the session state shape consistent. `_execute_action('generate_cv')` unpacks these strings into the `customizations` dict immediately before calling `generate_cv()`, so they only affect that one invocation and are not permanently mutated in state.
   258→
   259→**D5.4 — Section is hidden when no publications bib file is present.**
   260→`buildPublicationsReviewTable()` gets `recommendations: []` from the endpoint and calls `section.style.display = 'none'` rather than showing an empty table or an error. Users without a `publications.bib` file see an uncluttered Customisation step.
   261→
   262→**D5.5 — `venue_warning` surfaced as ⚠ tooltip in the DataTable.**
   263→Publications with no `journal` or `booktitle` field in BibTeX get a non-empty `venue_warning` string. The table cell renders this as a `⚠` span with `title` attribute so users can identify and exclude low-quality entries before generating the CV.
   264→
   265→### Test Results
   266→
   267→```
   268→Full suite: 236 passed, 1 warning in 4.53s
   269→```
   270→
   271→---
   272→
   273→## Phase 6 — Spell/Grammar Check
   274→
   275→**Status**: ✅ Complete (commit: see git log)
   276→
   277→### Changes Made
   278→
   279→| File | Change |
   280→|------|--------|
   281→| `scripts/utils/spell_checker.py` | New module — `SpellChecker` class wrapping `language_tool_python`; lazy JVM init; suppresses `SENTENCE_FRAGMENT`, `PUNCTUATION_PARAGRAPH`, `UPPERCASE_SENTENCE_START` for bullet context; skips all grammar checks for skill context; loads/saves `~/CV/custom_dictionary.json`; `prepopulate_from_skills()` auto-populates from master data |
   282→| `scripts/requirements.txt` | Added `language-tool-python>=3.3.0` |
   283→| `scripts/utils/conversation_manager.py` | `submit_rewrite_decisions()` now advances to `spell_check` phase (was `generation`); new `complete_spell_check(spell_audit)` method — stores `spell_audit[]` in state, advances to `generation`, returns `{flag_count, accepted_count, phase}` |
   284→| `scripts/web_app.py` | Imported `SpellChecker`; added lazy singleton `_spell_checker`; added `GET /api/spell-check-sections` (returns summary + approved-rewrite texts); added `POST /api/spell-check` (single-section check); added `GET /api/custom-dictionary`; added `POST /api/custom-dictionary`; added `POST /api/spell-check-complete` (saves audit, calls `complete_spell_check`) |
   285→| `web/index.html` | Added `tab-spell` tab between Rewrites and CV Editor; removed `step-spell` from UPCOMING set; updated `updateWorkflowSteps()` to handle `spell_check` phase and mark spell step done when phase is `generation`/`refinement`; added `spell_check` to `phaseToStep` map; added `spell` to `stepToTab` map; changed post-rewrite submission to navigate to spell tab instead of calling `generate_cv`; added `populateSpellCheckTab()`, `completeSpellCheckFastPath()`, `renderSpellSuggestions()`, `applySpellReplacement()`, `dismissSpellSuggestion()`, `addSpellWord()`, `submitSpellCheckDecisions()` |
   286→| `tests/test_conversation_manager.py` | Updated `test_phase_advances_to_generation` + `test_empty_decisions_returns_zeros` to expect `'spell_check'`; added `TestCompleteSpellCheck` class with 5 tests |
   287→
   288→### Design Decisions (Phase 6)
   289→
   290→**D6.1 — Lazy JVM init on `SpellChecker._get_tool()`.**
   291→LanguageTool starts a local Java process. Doing this at import/app-init time would add several seconds to startup and fail if Java is unavailable. Lazy init on the first `/api/spell-check` call keeps startup fast and allows graceful degradation.
   292→
   293→**D6.2 — `phase: spell_check` inserted between `rewrite_review` and `generation`.**
   294→The plan calls for a dedicated spell-check workflow step. Rather than running spell check inside the `generate_cv` action (which would block cancellation or re-run), it's a separate phase with its own `complete_spell_check()` transition method. The `generate_cv` action doesn't check the incoming phase, so it still works when called from `spell_check` directly (fast-path).
   295→
   296→**D6.3 — Zero-flag fast-path auto-advances without rendering the panel.**
   297→If all sections return 0 suggestions, `populateSpellCheckTab()` immediately calls `/api/spell-check-complete` with an empty audit, shows a brief success message, refreshes status, and calls `generate_cv`. No user interaction is required.
   298→
   299→**D6.4 — Sections are fetched from session state, not from a pre-generation render.**
   300→The spell check runs on: (a) the professional summary from master data, and (b) the `proposed` text of every approved rewrite. This is the text the user has already seen and approved — checking it prevents typos introduced during edit mode. Post-generation HTML text is not re-checked (that would require parsing the output, added complexity without clear benefit).
   301→
   302→**D6.5 — Custom dictionary pre-populates from skill names.**
   303→Technical skill names (e.g. "scikit-learn", "dplyr", "GWAS") would generate false MORFOLOGIK flags. `_prepopulate_spell_dict()` (called on first `/api/spell-check` request) loads all skills from `master_data` + the candidate's name into the custom dictionary in `~/CV/custom_dictionary.json`.
   304→
   305→### Test Results
   306→
   307→```
   308→Full suite: 241 passed, 1 warning in 4.84s  (+5 new tests)
   309→```
   310→
   311→---
   312→
   313→## Phase 7 — ATS Validation Report + Page Count
   314→
   315→**Status**: ✅ Complete (commit: `d9f284b`) | **Completed**: 2026-03-11
   316→
   317→### Changes Made
   318→
   319→| File | Change |
   320→|------|--------|
   321→| `scripts/utils/cv_orchestrator.py` | New module-level `validate_ats_report(output_dir, job_analysis)` function (~180 lines) running 16 checks: DOCX text selectable, zero tables, zero shapes, contact in body, standard headings, Heading 1 style, consistent date formats, ATS keyword presence, publications-heading exact match; HTML JSON-LD present/valid/knowsAbout/required-fields; WeasyPrint renders without error; WeasyPrint clipping warnings; PDF US Letter dimensions (612×792 pts via pypdf); returns `(checks, page_count)` |
   322→| `scripts/web_app.py` | Updated import to `from utils.cv_orchestrator import CVOrchestrator, validate_ats_report`; added `GET /api/ats-validate` endpoint — calls `validate_ats_report()`, caches `page_count` in session state, returns `{ok, checks, page_count, summary: {pass, warn, fail}}` |
   323→| `web/index.html` | Overhauled `populateDownloadTab()` (now `async`); fetches `/api/ats-validate`; displays page-count badge (amber if `<1.5` or `>3` pages); renders collapsible per-check table with ✓/⚠/✕ icons; blocks download buttons per failure format (DOCX/HTML/PDF blocked independently; `ats_keyword_presence` fail blocks all formats); updated 2 callers to `await populateDownloadTab()` |
   324→
   325→### Design Decisions (Phase 7)
   326→
   327→**D7.1 — `validate_ats_report()` is a module-level function, not a class method.**
   328→Validation operates on generated file paths, not on `CVOrchestrator` instance state. A module-level function is easier to unit test in isolation and does not require a fully initialised `CVOrchestrator` (which loads master data and LLM client).
   329→
   330→**D7.2 — Format-specific blocking with one cross-format exception.**
   331→DOCX-specific failures block only the DOCX download; HTML/JSON-LD failures block only the HTML download; PDF failures block only the PDF download. The `ats_keyword_presence` check (format='all') blocks all downloads because the content is shared: if keywords are missing from the generated text, they are missing in every output format.
   332→
   333→**D7.3 — Page count via WeasyPrint render, not PDF page count.**
   334→Using `weasyprint.HTML(string=html).render().pages` avoids a second PDF parse pass (pypdf is still used for the US Letter dimension check). The WeasyPrint render is already needed for checks 13 and 15 (render-without-error + clipping warnings), so `page_count` is a free byproduct.
   335→
   336→**D7.4 — Publications heading check requires exactly "Publications" in ATS DOCX.**
   337→ATS parsers target the standard heading label "Publications". The ATS DOCX must always use "Publications" exactly. The human-readable template heading is **conditional**: `"Publications"` when all publications are included; `"Selected Publications"` when a subset is included. The count `(N of M)` is never rendered. The check fails if the ATS DOCX contains a "publications" heading that is not exactly "Publications". _(Amendment 2026-03-11: human DOCX/HTML heading made conditional; (N of M) count notation removed.)_
   338→
   339→**D7.5 — Date-format consistency checks Mon YYYY vs MM/YYYY only.**
   340→The spec says mixed formats within one document are flagged. The em-dash vs en-dash vs hyphen separator is not enforced (too many false positives from different OS clipboard sources). Only structural format mixing (month-name format alongside numeric format) is flagged as fail.
   341→
   342→### Test Results
   343→
   344→```
   345→Full suite: 241 passed, 1 warning in 4.74s  (no new tests needed — validate_ats_report tested via test_cv_orchestrator.py existing tests)
   346→```
   347→
   348→---
   349→
   350→## Phase 8 — Phase Re-entry / Iterative Refinement
   351→
   352→**Status**: ✅ Complete (commit: `bf26797`) | **Completed**: 2026-03-11 | **Tests**: 252/252 passed
   353→
   354→### Changes Made
   355→
   356→| File | Change |
   357→|------|--------|
   358→| `scripts/utils/conversation_manager.py` | Added `_STEP_TO_PHASE` class dict mapping frontend step labels to internal phase strings. Added `back_to_phase(target_phase)` — sets `state['phase']`, `iterating`, `reentry_phase` without clearing any downstream state; saves session. Added `re_run_phase(target_phase)` — builds `_downstream_context()` string (prior approvals, omitted/emphasised experiences/skills, accepted spell-check fixes) and passes as `_prior_context` in `user_prefs`; re-executes LLM call for analysis/customization/rewrite_review; stores prior and new outputs; saves session. |
   359→| `scripts/web_app.py` | Added `POST /api/back-to-phase` endpoint wrapping `conversation.back_to_phase()`. Added `POST /api/re-run-phase` endpoint wrapping `conversation.re_run_phase()`; returns 400 if call returns `ok: False`. Updated `/api/status` to return `iterating` and `reentry_phase` fields. |
   360→| `web/index.html` | Added `backToPhase(step)`, `confirmReRunPhase(step)`, `reRunPhase(step)` JS functions. Added "Iterative Refinement" panel at end of `populateDownloadTab()` with three buttons (Refine Customisations, Refine Rewrites, Re-analyse Job). Overhauled `updateWorkflowSteps()`: sets `el.innerHTML` to inject hover-visible `↻` rerun span inside completed steps, amber "↻ Refining" badge on re-entered step when `status.iterating` is true; injected `<style>` for `.step.completed:hover .step-rerun { opacity:1 }`. |
   361→| `tests/test_conversation_manager.py` | Added `TestBackToPhase` (6 tests) and `TestReRunPhase` (5 tests). Fixed `TestReRunPhase.setUp` to configure `mock_llm.recommend_customizations.return_value` and `mock_llm.analyze_job_description.return_value` as dicts to avoid `MagicMock` JSON-serialisation error in `_save_session`. |
   362→
   363→### Design Decisions (Phase 8)
   364→
   365→**D8.1 — `back_to_phase` accepts both frontend step labels and internal phase strings.**
   366→`_STEP_TO_PHASE.get(target_phase, target_phase)` passes through unrecognised strings unchanged. This means the frontend can pass either `'customizations'` (UI label) or `'customization'` (internal) and both resolve correctly.
   367→
   368→**D8.2 — Downstream state is never cleared by back-navigation.**
   369→Per the approved plan decision. `back_to_phase` only mutates `state['phase']`, `state['iterating']`, and `state['reentry_phase']`. All generated files, approved rewrites, experience/skill decisions, and spell-check audit survive the transition. This lets the LLM improve on the last pass rather than starting fresh.
   370→
   371→**D8.3 — `_downstream_context()` extracted to `_build_downstream_context(self)` class method.**
   372→Originally implemented as a nested closure. Amended 2026-03-11: extract to a private class method `_build_downstream_context(self)` so it can be unit tested in isolation. Add `TestBuildDownstreamContext` tests to `tests/test_conversation_manager.py`.
   373→
   374→**D8.4 — Re-run sets `phase = 'customization'` for both analysis and customization re-runs.**
   375→After re-running analysis, the user should proceed through customisation again (their prior selections may no longer apply). Setting phase to `'customization'` rather than back to `'job_analysis'` means the frontend lands the user in the right next step without an extra click.
   376→
   377→**D8.5 — ↻ rerun icon is injected into `el.innerHTML`, not a child element.**
   378→`updateWorkflowSteps` previously set only `el.classList`. Switching to `el.innerHTML` allows injecting the `<span class="step-rerun">↻</span>` inside the step element. The span's `onclick` uses `event.stopPropagation()` to prevent the enclosing `handleStepClick` from also firing.
   379→
   380→### Test Results
   381→
   382→```
   383→tests/test_conversation_manager.py  ...  252 passed  (+11 new tests)
   384→Full suite: 252 passed, 1 warning in 5.04s
   385→```
   386→
   387→---
   388→
   389→## Phase 9 — Skills Canonicalisation + Bullet Reordering
   390→
   391→**Status**: ✅ Complete (commit: `6c96ea5`) | **Completed**: 2026-03-11 | **Tests**: 272/272 passed
   392→
   393→### Changes Made
   394→
   395→| File | Change |
   396→|------|--------|
   397→| `scripts/data/synonym_map.json` | New file. 80 ML/data-science/CS term mappings used for bidirectional ATS keyword expansion and alias deduplication (`ML`→`Machine Learning`, `NLP`, `GCP`, `k8s`, `sklearn`, …). |
   398→| `scripts/utils/cv_orchestrator.py` | Added `_load_synonym_map()` + lookup indices (`_synonym_map`, `_canonical_index`, `_expansion_index`) loaded in `__init__`. Added `canonical_skill_name(name)` public method. `_organize_skills_by_category()`: deduplicates skills by canonical name — merges `ML` + `Machine Learning` into one entry, keeps higher-years entry, populates `aliases[]`. `_optimize_skills_for_ats()`: expands both ATS keywords and required_skills via synonym map before scoring so bidirectional abbreviation/full-form matching works. `_select_content_hybrid()`: new per-experience bullet ordering block — auto-sorts `achievements` by token+synonym overlap with `ats_keywords`; applies explicit `customizations['achievement_orders']` (list of original indices) when present; stores result as `exp['ordered_achievements']`. |
   399→| `scripts/utils/conversation_manager.py` | In `generate_cv` action: inject `state.get('achievement_orders', {})` into `customizations` before calling `orchestrator.generate_cv()`. |
   400→| `scripts/web_app.py` | `GET /api/synonym-lookup?term=X` → `{term, canonical, found}`. `GET /api/synonym-map` → full map. `POST /api/reorder-bullets` → accepts `{experience_id, order:[int...]}` and persists in `state['achievement_orders']`; empty `order` resets to relevance-sorted. |
   401→| `templates/cv-template.html` | Skills: render `skill.aliases` list in a `<span class="skill-alias">` with tooltip on both page-2 and page-3 skill columns. Experience bullets: use `exp.ordered_achievements` if defined, else fall back to `exp.achievements`, on both page-2 and page-3 experience loops. |
   402→| `web/index.html` | ↕ button added to each experience row in the customisation table. `showBulletReorder(expId, expTitle)`: fetches achievements for experience, builds modal with numbered list and ↑/↓ row-swap buttons. `moveBullet()`, `_updateBulletArrows()`, `saveBulletOrder()` (calls `/api/reorder-bullets`), `resetBulletOrder()` (passes empty order to reset). |
   403→| `tests/test_cv_orchestrator.py` | Added `TestSynonymMap` (7), `TestOptimizeSkillsWithSynonyms` (4), `TestOrganizeSkillsAlias` (5), `TestBulletOrderInSelectContent` (4) — 20 new tests. |
   404→
   405→### Design Decisions (Phase 9)
   406→
   407→**D9.1 — Synonym map is a flat JSON file, not hard-coded.**
   408→`scripts/data/synonym_map.json` is auto-loaded at orchestrator startup and can be extended without touching Python. A missing file degrades gracefully to `{}`.
   409→
   410→**D9.2 — Synonym matching is bidirectional via a single expansion index.**
   411→Both directions (alias→canonical and canonical→alias) are added to `_expansion_index`. ATS keywords are expanded before scoring, so a skill named `ML` matches keyword `Machine Learning` and vice versa. Terminology never changes — only matching.
   412→
   413→**D9.3 — Alias deduplication merges entries but preserves original names as aliases.**
   414→When `ML` and `Machine Learning` both appear in master data, the merge produces a single entry named `Machine Learning` (canonical) with `aliases: ['ML']`. The original name is not lost and appears in the CV template's tooltip.
   415→
   416→**D9.4 — Auto bullet sorting defaults to keyword-token overlap.**
   417→No LLM call is needed. A closure counts how many tokens (after synonym expansion) in each bullet text overlap with `ats_keywords`. This is fast, deterministic, and auditable. User-explicit ordering always takes precedence.
   418→
   419→**D9.5 — User bullet ordering is stored outside `customizations`.**
   420→`state['achievement_orders']` is persisted alongside spell-check and rewrite audit data. It is injected into `customizations` just before `generate_cv()` so the orchestrator receives it cleanly without polluting the LLM-facing recommendation structure.
   421→
   422→### Test Results
   423→
   424→```
   425→tests/test_cv_orchestrator.py  ...  272 passed  (+20 new tests)
   426→Full suite: 272 passed, 1 warning in 4.33s
   427→```

```text
     1→# CV-Builder: Comprehensive Project Specification
     2→## Version 1.0 MVP - February 11, 2026
     3→
     4→---
     5→
     6→## Executive Summary
     7→
     8→**Project**: LLM-Driven CV Generation System  
     9→**Timeline**: 1 week (Feb 11-18, 2026)  
    10→**Owner**: Gregory R. Warnes  
    11→**Status**: Specification Complete - Ready for Implementation
    12→
    13→### Mission Statement
    14→Create an AI-powered system that generates customized, professional CVs tailored to specific job descriptions in minutes, not hours.
    15→
    16→### Core Value Proposition
    17→> "Ability to generate customized resumes based on job description"
    18→
    19→The system analyzes job postings using LLM intelligence, recommends relevant experience and skills, allows user review and editing, then generates both ATS-optimized and human-readable CVs.
    20→
    21→---
    22→
    23→## 1. Project Scope
    24→
    25→### 1.1 In-Scope for v1.0 MVP
    26→
    27→#### **Priority #1: CV Editing UI**
    28→- In-browser editing of generated CV content
    29→- Real-time preview of changes
    30→- Ability to tweak selected experiences, skills, achievements
    31→- Save edits before final download
    32→
    33→#### **Priority #2: Document Generation**
    34→Four formats are generated per CV run:
    35→- **HTML** (`*.html`): Jinja2 renders `templates/cv-template.html` → self-contained HTML with embedded CSS (2-column layout) and a Schema.org/Person JSON-LD `<script>` block in `<head>` for structured-data ATS parsing. Directly previewable in-browser.
    36→- **PDF** (`*.pdf`): WeasyPrint (primary) / Chrome headless (fallback) converts the rendered HTML → PDF. Fonts embedded, background colours preserved.
    37→- **ATS DOCX** (`*_ATS.docx`): python-docx generated, single-column, plain-text, keyword-optimized per ATS guidelines.
    38→- **Human DOCX** (`*.docx`): docxtpl (Jinja2) renders `templates/cv-template.docx` → Word-native editable DOCX (Calibri, standard margins). Independently styled from the PDF — no visual parity requirement.
    39→
    40→#### **Core Workflow** (Already Implemented):
    41→- Job description upload/paste
    42→- LLM semantic analysis (GitHub Models)
    43→- Customization recommendations
    44→- Interactive review tables (DataTables)
    45→- Session persistence
    46→
    47→### 1.2 Out of Scope (Phase 2)
    48→
    49→**Deferred Features** (ranked #3-#10):
    50→- Cover letter generation
    51→- Interview screening question responses
    52→- Job application tracking
    53→- ~~Google Drive integration~~ — **dropped** (git-only archiving; no Drive integration planned)
    54→- Analytics and insights
    55→- LinkedIn profile generation
    56→- Mobile application
    57→- Multi-user support+ (single-user MVP)
    58→
    59→---
    60→
    61→## 2. User Stories & Acceptance Criteria
    62→
    63→### US-1: Generate Customized CV from Job Description
    64→**As a** job applicant  
    65→**I want to** paste a job description and get a tailored CV  
    66→**So that** I can apply quickly with relevant, optimized content
    67→
    68→**Acceptance Criteria**:
    69→- ✅ System analyzes job description within 10 seconds
    70→- ✅ Presents recommended experiences/skills for review
    71→- ✅ Allows approval/rejection of recommendations
    72→- ✅ Generates both PDF and DOCX formats
    73→- ✅ Downloadable files ready for submission
    74→
    75→### US-2: Edit Generated CV Content
    76→**As a** user reviewing my generated CV  
    77→**I want to** make final edits in the application  
    78→**So that** I can perfect the content before downloading
    79→
    80→**Acceptance Criteria**:
    81→- ✅ Edit experience descriptions, dates, achievements
    82→- ✅ Add/remove bullet points
    83→- ✅ Reorder sections
    84→- ✅ See live preview of changes
    85→- ✅ Changes persist in session
    86→- ✅ Re-generate documents with edits applied
    87→
    88→### US-3: Optimize for ATS Systems
    89→**As a** job applicant  
    90→**I want** my CV to be ATS-parseable  
    91→**So that** it passes automated screening
    92→
    93→**Acceptance Criteria**:
    94→- ✅ DOCX format is ATS-compatible (per REQUIREMENTS.md guidelines)
    95→- ✅ Keywords from job description incorporated naturally
    96→- ✅ Clean single-column structure
    97→- ✅ Standard section headings
    98→- ✅ No tables, graphics, or complex formatting in ATS version
    99→
   100→### US-4: Create Human-Readable CV
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>

 `./tasks/user-story-applicant.md`

```text
     1→# User Story: Job Applicant Perspective
     2→**Persona:** Gregory R. Warnes — senior scientist/leader applying to a specific role  
     3→**Scope:** Full workflow from job discovery through application submission  
     4→**Format:** End-to-end narrative with numbered steps, acceptance criteria, and UI interaction notes
     5→
     6→---
     7→
     8→## US-A1: Discover and Queue a Job Opportunity
     9→
    10→**As a** job applicant,  
    11→**I want to** paste or import a job posting URL or text so the system can begin tailoring my application,  
    12→**So that** I spend no time on manual transcription and can start the process immediately.
    13→
    14→**Precondition:** `Master_CV_Data.json` is up to date.
    15→
    16→**Steps:**
    17→1. Open the web UI and start a new session.
    18→2. Paste the job posting URL (e.g., from LinkedIn, Indeed, Glassdoor) **or** paste the full job description text.
    19→3. If a URL is supplied, the system fetches and extracts the plain-text job description, warning me if the site is login-protected (LinkedIn, Indeed) so I can manually copy the text.
    20→4. The system confirms the company name, role title, and date extracted from the posting.
    21→5. I confirm or correct those values.
    22→6. The session is saved with `status: "queued"` so I can return later.
    23→
    24→**Acceptance Criteria:**
    25→- URL and paste-text paths both work.
    26→- Protected-site warning surfaced with a manual-copy fallback.
    27→- Company name, role title, and date auto-extracted and editable.
    28→- Session persisted immediately after step 5.
    29→
    30→---
    31→
    32→## US-A2: Understand What the Job Requires
    33→
    34→**As a** job applicant,  
    35→**I want to** see a structured analysis of the job description — required vs. preferred qualifications, key keywords, and domain focus —  
    36→**So that** I know what the employer is prioritising before I approve any customisations.
    37→
    38→**Steps:**
    39→1. After job text is confirmed, the system runs LLM analysis; a progress indicator is shown within 1 s of starting (target completion ≤10 s under normal load — this is a performance guideline, not a hard SLA).
    40→2. The full `Master_CV_Data.json` is included in the LLM context alongside the job description so the analysis can identify mismatches as well as matches.
    41→3. The UI displays:
    42→   - Required qualifications (must-have)
    43→   - Preferred qualifications (nice-to-have)
    44→   - Extracted keywords ranked by frequency/importance
    45→   - Inferred domain focus (e.g., "Genomics/Research", "ML Engineering", "Leadership")
    46→   - Inferred role type (IC vs. leadership, seniority level)
    47→   - **Apparent mismatches** between the job requirements and the master CV data (e.g., a required skill not evidenced, a seniority level higher than current title)
    48→4. The system asks clarifying questions arising from both general ambiguity and specific mismatches. For example:
    49→   - "Do you want to emphasise leadership or technical IC aspects?"
    50→   - "Should publications be included? This appears to be a research role. Based on the job requirements, the following publications look most relevant: [ranked list]. Shall I include all, a subset, or none?"
    51→   - "Kubernetes is listed as required but isn’t in your master data — do you have relevant experience to add, or should we note this gap?"
    52→5. I answer the clarifying questions (UI: dropdown or button choices, not free text).  
    53→6. If additional clarification is needed, return to 4.
    54→7. Analysis is saved to session.
    55→
    56→**Acceptance Criteria:**
    57→- Required/preferred split displayed clearly.
    58→- Mismatch analysis run against master CV data; at least one mismatch surfaced as a clarifying question when a required skill or role-type signal has no evidence in the master data.
    59→- At least one clarifying question surfaced when domain/role-type is ambiguous.
    60→- My answers to clarifying questions persist in session state and in `metadata.json` under `clarification_answers`.
    61→- Clarification answers are passed as context to all downstream LLM calls in this session (cover letter, screening responses, iterative refinement) — I am never asked to re-state a preference I already gave.
    62→- If a prior session exists for the same role type, my previous clarification answers are pre-populated as defaults (editable before confirming).
    63→- Analysis results survive browser refresh (session-backed).
    64→
    65→---
    66→
    67→## US-A3: Review and Approve Content Customisations
    68→
    69→**As a** job applicant,  
    70→**I want to** review which experiences, achievements, and skills the system recommends including — with relevance scores —  
    71→**So that** I have full control over what enters my CV before any document is generated.
    72→
    73→**Steps:**
    74→1. System presents content recommendations in interactive tables (DataTables):
    75→   - **Experiences**:
    76→      - reverse chronological order by default, I can reorder them using up down buttons.
    77→      - each entry with relevance score, proposed bullet ordering, accept/reject toggle
    78→   - **Selected Achievements**: ordered and ranked by relevance, accept/reject, reorder using up and down buttons
    79→   - **Skills**: recommended skill groups and individual skills, ordered and ranked by relevance, accept/reject, reorder using up and down buttons
    80→   - **Selected Publications**: ranked list of publications recommended by the LLM from `publications.bib`, each with a relevance score and rationale (keyword overlap, domain match, first-author status, recency); accept/reject per item; reorder using up/down buttons; section omitted entirely if no items accepted.
    81→   - **Sections to omit**: e.g., "Omit Selected Publications — industry role", with rationale.
    82→2. I adjust toggles — include/exclude any item.
    83→3. I can reorder experience bullets within an entry by drag-and-drop or up/down controls.
    84→4. I click **Confirm Customisations** to advance.
    85→5. Session saves my decisions.
    86→
    87→**Acceptance Criteria:**
    88→- Every recommended item shows a relevance score and brief rationale.
    89→- Include/exclude toggles work for experiences, achievements, skills, and publications individually.
    90→- Up/down buttons for experiences, achievements, skills, and publications to change order.
    91→- Bullet reordering within a job entry is supported.
    92→- "Omit" suggestions (sections, experiences) are explained, not silently dropped.
    93→- LLM-recommended publications list is shown whenever `publications.bib` is non-empty; the list is pre-ranked by relevance to the current job and each item shows its relevance score and rationale.
    94→- If all publications are rejected, the "Selected Publications" section is omitted from the CV entirely (not rendered as an empty section).
    95→- Confirmed decisions (including publication selections) persist in session and in `metadata.json` under `clarification_answers.selected_publications`.
    96→
    97→---
    98→
    99→## US-A4: Review and Approve Text Rewrites
   100→
   101→**As a** job applicant,  
   102→**I want to** see a card-based before/after diff for each LLM-proposed text rewrite — and accept, edit, or reject each one individually —  
   103→**So that** no modified text enters my CV without my explicit approval.
   104→
   105→**Steps:**
   106→1. After content customisations are confirmed, the system calls the LLM to propose rewrites.
   107→2. The UI displays one card per proposal, each showing:
   108→   - **Before** text (greyed/strikethrough)
   109→   - **After** text (highlighted)
   110→   - Keywords introduced (pill badges)
   111→   - Collapsible rationale + evidence citation
   112→   - **Accept / Edit / Reject** buttons
   113→3. For `skill_add` proposals with `evidence_strength == "weak"`, a prominent "⚠ Candidate to confirm" badge appears.
   114→4. **Edit** replaces the After text with an inline textarea pre-filled with the proposed text; I save my version.
   115→5. A sticky summary bar shows accepted / rejected / pending counts.
   116→6. **Submit All Decisions** is disabled until every card is actioned.
   117→7. I click Submit → session advances to generation phase.
   118→
   119→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   120→- Every proposal has a visible before/after diff.
   121→- Weak-evidence skill additions are badged and cannot be silently accepted without noticing the warning.
   122→- Edited final text is what actually enters the CV — not the original LLM proposal.
   123→- Submit is blocked until all cards are actioned.
   124→- Rewrite audit (proposal + outcome + final text) is persisted in session.
   125→
   126→---
   127→
   128→## US-A4b: Spell & Grammar Check Before Generation
   129→
   130→**As a** job applicant,  
   131→**I want to** review and resolve any spelling or grammar flags on my finalised CV text before generating documents,  
   132→**So that** the delivered files contain no embarrassing errors — and every correction is my explicit decision.
   133→
   134→**Precondition:** All rewrite decisions from US-A4 have been submitted.
   135→
   136→**Steps:**
   137→1. The system runs LanguageTool on all finalized text fields (summary, bullets, cover letter if present, screening responses if present).
   138→2. If **no flags** are found, the UI shows a green banner: "✅ No spelling or grammar issues found" and I proceed directly to generation.
   139→3. If flags exist, the UI displays a checklist of flagged items. Each item shows:
   140→   - The flagged text (highlighted in context)
   141→   - The suggestion
   142→   - The context type (`summary` / `bullet` / `skill_name` / `cover_letter` / `screening_response`)
   143→4. For each flag I choose one of:
   144→   - **Accept** — apply the suggestion
   145→   - **Reject** — leave as-is (the flag is dismissed)
   146→   - **Edit** — open an inline textarea for my own correction
   147→   - **Add to Dictionary** — suppress this word in all future sessions
   148→5. Once every flag is resolved, **Proceed to Generation** becomes active.
   149→6. I click Proceed → session advances to generation phase; spell audit is written to session state.
   150→
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-03-11 18:18:13Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./tasks/user-story-applicant.md`

```text
   150→
   151→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   152→- `bullet` and `skill_name` context types do not generate sentence-fragment or missing-subject warnings.
   153→- Proper nouns and technical terms already in `custom_dictionary.json` produce no flags.
   154→- Words added to dictionary via **Add to Dictionary** are immediately suppressed in the current session and persist to `~/CV/custom_dictionary.json` for future sessions.
   155→- Editing a flag applies my text, not the LLM suggestion.
   156→- **Proceed to Generation** is blocked while any flag remains unresolved.
   157→- Spell audit (flagged text, suggestion, outcome, final) is persisted in session and included in `metadata.json`.
   158→- Zero-flag case completes instantly — no unnecessary blocking step.
   159→
   160→---
   161→
   162→## US-A5a: Generate HTML for Layout Review
   163→
   164→**As a** job applicant,  
   165→**I want to** generate the HTML version of my CV immediately after approving rewrites,  
   166→**So that** I can review and refine the visual layout before committing to PDF and ATS outputs.
   167→
   168→**Steps:**
   169→1. After spell/grammar check decisions are submitted, I click **Generate HTML Preview**.
   170→2. The system shows a progress indicator: "Applying rewrites… Rendering HTML…"
   171→3. On completion, the HTML file is written to the archive directory and the inline preview opens automatically:
   172→   - `CV_{Company}_{Role}_{Date}.html` — self-contained browser-previewable file; contains Schema.org JSON-LD metadata in `<head>`
   173→4. `metadata.json` is created/updated with the current `rewrite_audit` and `spell_audit` arrays.
   174→5. The UI transitions to the **HTML Layout Review** step (US-A5b).
   175→
   176→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   177→- Only the HTML format is generated at this step; PDF and ATS DOCX are not yet produced.
   178→- HTML preview opens automatically in the inline preview pane.
   179→- HTML preview renders correctly in any browser with two-column layout and styling intact.
   180→- A progress indicator is shown within 1 s of clicking Generate HTML Preview; generation typically completes within 30 s under normal load (performance guideline, not a hard SLA).
   181→- Errors surface as user-visible messages, not silent failures.
   182→- Archive directory and `metadata.json` created at this step.
   183→
   184→---
   185→
   186→## US-A5b: Review and Refine HTML Layout
   187→
   188→**As a** job applicant,  
   189→**I want to** preview the generated HTML and issue natural-language layout instructions to the LLM,  
   190→**So that** I can fix section order, page-break placement, and structural presentation without editing HTML by hand.
   191→
   192→**Steps:**
   193→1. After HTML generation (US-A5a) completes, the UI opens an **HTML Preview** pane alongside a **Layout Instructions** text prompt.
   194→2. I review the rendered HTML (inline preview or browser link) and identify structural issues:
   195→   - Section out of order (e.g., Publications appearing before Skills)
   196→   - An experience entry spilling across a page break awkwardly
   197→   - A section I want moved to page 2
   198→   - Too much whitespace at the bottom of page 1
   199→3. I type a plain-English instruction in the Layout Instructions field, e.g.:
   200→   - *"Move Publications to after the Skills section."*
   201→   - *"Keep the Genentech entry on one page — don't split it across pages."*
   202→   - *"Move the Selected Achievements section to page 2."*
   203→   - *"Reduce spacing between bullet points to fit on 2 pages."*
   204→4. I click **Apply Layout Changes**. The LLM interprets the instruction and modifies the HTML template/CSS or section order accordingly.
   205→5. The HTML preview refreshes with the updated layout.
   206→6. I can issue additional layout instructions; each is applied incrementally.
   207→7. When satisfied, I click **Confirm Layout**. The final HTML is saved to the archive and the workflow advances to **US-A5c: Generate Final Output**.
   208→8. Layout instructions are stored in `metadata.json` under `layout_instructions` (array of strings, in order applied).
   209→
   210→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   211→- The HTML preview pane opens automatically on entry from US-A5a.
   212→- The Layout Instructions field accepts free-text and sends it to the LLM as a structured layout-edit prompt.
   213→- Example instruction types include (but are not limited to): section reordering, section relocation (page 1 vs page 2), page-break hints, and spacing adjustments.
   214→- Each applied instruction updates only the structural/presentational layer — approved rewrite text is never altered.
   215→- The preview refreshes after each instruction is applied.
   216→- **Confirm Layout** saves the final HTML and triggers US-A5c; it does NOT generate PDF/DOCX directly.
   217→- All applied layout instructions are recorded in `metadata.json` under `layout_instructions`.
   218→- If the LLM cannot confidently interpret an instruction, it asks clarifying questions rather than silently applying a guess.
   219→
   220→---
   221→
   222→## US-A5c: Generate Final Output (PDF + ATS DOCX)
   223→
   224→**As a** job applicant,  
   225→**I want to** generate the PDF and ATS DOCX from the layout-confirmed HTML,  
   226→**So that** my submission-ready files faithfully reflect the reviewed and approved layout.
   227→
   228→**Steps:**
   229→1. After clicking **Proceed to Final Generation** in US-A5b, the system generates the remaining two formats from the final HTML:
   230→   - `CV_{Company}_{Role}_{Date}.pdf` — rendered from the confirmed HTML; fonts embedded, full visual styling
   231→   - `CV_{Company}_{Role}_{Date}_ATS.docx` — plain-text single-column; keyword-optimized for ATS portals
   232→2. The system shows a progress indicator: "Converting to PDF… Generating ATS DOCX…"
   233→3. On completion, the UI shows download links for all three formats (HTML already in archive from US-A5a/b).
   234→4. `metadata.json` is updated with the final `layout_instructions` array and generation timestamps.
   235→
   236→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   237→- PDF and ATS DOCX are generated from the HTML that was confirmed in US-A5b (no re-render from scratch).
   238→- File naming follows `CV_{CompanyName}_{Role}_{Date}` convention; ATS file adds `_ATS` suffix.
   239→- All three formats available as download links on completion.
   240→- A progress indicator is shown within 1 s of clicking Proceed to Final Generation; generation typically completes within 45 s under normal load (performance guideline, not a hard SLA).
   241→- Errors surface as user-visible messages, not silent failures.
   242→- `metadata.json` updated with generation timestamps for each format.
   243→
   244→---
   245→
   246→## US-A6: Review and Iteratively Refine Generated Output
   247→
   248→**As a** job applicant,  
   249→**I want to** preview the generated files and request targeted edits before finalising,
```
</details></tool-use>

 path `./tasks/user-story-ux-expert.md` pattern `natural.?language.*layout|layout instruction|US-A5b`

```text
216-2. **Processing feedback** — After submitting an instruction, the UI must show a processing indicator and then refresh the preview. A blank wait or a full-page reload is not acceptable.
217-3. **Change attribution** — After an instruction is applied, the UI should display a brief confirmation of what was changed (e.g., "Publications section moved after Skills"), not just silently update the preview.
218-4. **Clarification handling** — If the LLM cannot confidently apply an instruction, it must ask a clarifying question inline — not silently apply a guess or display an error.
219-5. **Instruction history** — A visible log of applied instructions in this session allows the user to review what has been changed before proceeding. Each entry in the log has an **Undo** action.
220-6. **Single proceed action** — The button to advance to PDF/ATS generation should be a single, clearly labelled **Proceed to Final Generation** — equally usable whether the user made zero or many layout changes. No "Skip" vs. "Confirm" ambiguity.
221:7. **Content safety assurance** — The UI must communicate clearly (label, notice, or tooltip) that applying layout instructions cannot alter approved rewrite text.
222-
223-**Failure Modes to Guard Against:**
224-- Instruction submitted, preview unchanged, no feedback — user does not know if it worked.
225-- LLM silently applies a wrong interpretation of an ambiguous instruction.
226-- No history of applied instructions — user cannot review or undo individual steps before proceeding.
```
</details></tool-use>

Perfect! I now have all the information needed. Let me compile my comprehensive findings:

## Summary: Phase 12 (Layout Instructions) and GAP-14 (Workflow Progress Indicator)

### 1. GAP-14: Workflow Progress Indicator

**Location:** `/Users/warnes/src/cv-builder/tasks/gaps.md` lines 278-300

**What GAP-14 Requires:**
A persistent, visible workflow progress indicator showing named stages with:
- Clear visual distinction between completed, active, and upcoming stages
- Named stages: Job Input → Analysis → Review → Generate → Layout → Finalise
- Back-navigation that preserves previously approved content with explicit confirmation for destructive nav
- Session restoration showing the last active stage with populated data
- Stage indicator updates without full page reload

**Current Status:**
- ✅ **COMPLETED in Phase 2** — The workflow progress bar has been implemented with 8 steps: 📥 Job Input → 🔍 Analysis → ⚙️ Customise → ✏️ Rewrites → 🔤 Spell Check → 📄 Generate → 🎨 Layout → ✅ Finalise
- ✅ Spell Check and Layout steps show as "upcoming" (static `.upcoming` class)
- ✅ Back-navigation preserves downstream state
- ✅ Session lock prevents concurrent access

**Reference:** IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md lines 32-34, Phase 2 complete section

---

### 2. Phase 12: Natural-Language Layout Instructions

**Location:** 
- User story specs: `/Users/warnes/src/cv-builder/tasks/user-story-applicant.md` (US-A5a, US-A5b, US-A5c, lines 162-242)
- UX requirements: `/Users/warnes/src/cv-builder/tasks/user-story-ux-expert.md` (lines 211-226)
- IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md line 42 (Phase 12 pending, est. 2.5h)

**What Phase 12 Is Supposed to Do:**

Phase 12 implements **US-A5b: Review and Refine HTML Layout** — a natural-language layout customization workflow.

**Workflow (3-step process):**

1. **US-A5a: Generate HTML for Layout Review** (prerequisite)
   - System generates initial HTML from approved rewrites
   - Creates archive directory and `metadata.json`
   - Opens inline preview pane
   - Advances to layout review phase

2. **US-A5b: Natural-Language Layout Instructions** (Phase 12 core)
   - User reviews rendered HTML preview and identifies structural issues (section order, page breaks, spacing)
   - User types plain-English instructions, e.g.: `"Move Publications to after the Skills section"` or `"Keep the Genentech entry on one page"`
   - System sends instruction to LLM with structured prompt
   - LLM interprets and modifies HTML template/CSS or section order **without altering approved rewrite text**
   - Preview refreshes after each instruction
   - User can issue multiple instructions incrementally
   - All instructions logged to `metadata.json` under `layout_instructions[]`
   - UI shows instruction history with optional **Undo** per instruction

3. **US-A5c: Generate Final Output** (post-Phase 12)
   - System generates PDF and ATS DOCX from confirmed HTML
   - All three formats available for download

**Key Requirements:**
- Layout instructions must be **incremental** — each applied independently, updates only visual/structural layer
- **Text is never altered** by layout instructions — only approved rewrite text is used
- Instruction examples: section reordering, section relocation (page 1 vs. 2), page-break hints, spacing adjustments
- If LLM cannot confidently interpret an instruction, it asks **inline clarifying questions** (not errors)
- UI shows:
  - Processing indicator after submission
  - Confirmation of what was changed (e.g., "Publications section moved after Skills")
  - History log of applied instructions with undo capability
  - Scope label communicating that text is safe
- Single "Proceed to Final Generation" button (works whether user made zero or many changes)

---

### 3. Current Implementation State

**Phase 12 Prerequisites Completed:**
- ✅ Phase 5: HTML generation infrastructure (`_render_cv_html_pdf()`)
- ✅ Phase 6: Spell check (demonstrates LLM integration for post-generation edits)
- ✅ Phase 8: Phase re-entry/iterative refinement patterns
- ✅ Phase 10: Persuasion checks and progress tracking API

**Phase 12 Not Yet Started:**
- ❌ No `/api/apply-layout-instruction` endpoint
- ❌ No `layout_instructions` handling in `conversation_manager.py`
- ❌ No LLM prompt for layout edit interpretation
- ❌ No layout UI panel in `web/index.html` (tab exists but unmarked as upcoming)
- ❌ No instruction history/undo logic

---

### 4. Architectural Patterns to Reuse

**From Phase 6 (Spell Check):**
- Lazy module initialization pattern for external tools
- Zero-flag fast-path (no issues found → auto-advance)
- Session-backed audit trail pattern (`spell_audit[]`)
- Structured prompt for LLM interpretation (vs. free-form)
- API endpoint pattern: `POST /api/action` → `conversation.complete_action()` → `state['phase'] = 'next_phase'`

**From Phase 8 (Phase Re-entry):**
- `_build_downstream_context()` pattern for passing prior approvals as LLM context
- Phase transition with state preservation
- Frontend UI affordances for re-running stages

**From Template System:**
- `cv-template.html` uses Jinja2 variables for conditional sections
- Current structure: sidebar (left 32%) + main content (right 68%) on page 1; pages 2+ flow naturally
- Section-level CSS classes allow visual reordering via template variables
- `_prepare_cv_data_for_template()` builds data dict passed to Jinja2 renderer

**API Pattern Examples:**
- `/api/status` — returns current phase + metadata
- `/api/spell-check` — POST with section text → returns suggestions
- `/api/reorder-bullets` — POST with experience_id + order array → persists in state
- All routes follow pattern: accept input → call `conversation` method → return `{ok, phase, data}`

---

### 5. Questions Resolved from IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md

**Q5: "Layout instructions — scope of ATS DOCX"**
- Layout instructions apply to HTML generation and PDF rendering only
- ATS DOCX is generated separately **from the confirmed HTML** (not from scratch) in US-A5c
- ATS DOCX inherits text content from HTML but ignores all visual changes
- This is safe because ATS rules forbid multi-column layouts anyway; layout instructions only affect visual/structural layer

---

### 6. Key Files to Modify in Phase 12

| File | Change Type | Details |
|------|------------|---------|
| `scripts/utils/conversation_manager.py` | New phase & methods | Add `layout` phase; new `complete_layout_review(instructions[])` method; extract layout context for LLM |
| `scripts/utils/cv_orchestrator.py` | New method | `apply_layout_instruction(html_content, instruction, prior_instructions)` — calls LLM, returns updated HTML + confirmation |
| `scripts/web_app.py` | New endpoints | `POST /api/layout-instruction` — accept instruction, call orchestrator, return updated HTML + change summary; `POST /api/layout-complete` — advance phase; `GET /api/layout-history` — return applied instructions |
| `web/index.html` | New UI panel | Activate `#tab-layout`; add Layout Instructions textarea, HTML preview pane, instruction history log with undo buttons, "Proceed to Final Generation" button |
| `templates/cv-template.html` | Potentially none | Layout instructions modify via Jinja2 template variables (section order, spacing CSS), not template code |
| `tests/test_conversation_manager.py` | New tests | Test layout phase transitions, instruction context building, undo/redo logic |

---

This completes the search. Phase 12 is well-specified in the user stories, architecturally aligned with existing patterns, and ready for implementation once Phase 11 (Finalise & Archive) is complete.

```text
     1→# Frontend Refactoring: Modularize web/index.html
     2→
     3→## Context
     4→
     5→Phase 10 (Persuasion Checks + Loading State) has been completed successfully. However, the monolithic `web/index.html` file has grown to 5,484 lines (862 HTML, 763 CSS, 4,617 JS), making it difficult to:
     6→
     7→1. **Maintain and extend** — Finding relevant code requires scrolling through thousands of lines
     8→2. **Test in isolation** — No clear module boundaries for unit testing UI logic
     9→3. **Onboard contributors** — No clear separation of concerns or feature ownership
    10→4. **Debug effectively** — Function dependencies are implicit and scattered throughout
    11→
    12→This refactoring breaks `web/index.html` into **15 focused modules** (14 JS + 1 CSS file) organized by responsibility, with a clear entry point (`ui-core.js`) and explicit dependency graph. The refactoring preserves all functionality while enabling future feature development and testing.
    13→
    14→---
    15→
    16→## Design Decisions
    17→
    18→### D11.1 — Module Organization by Responsibility
    19→JavaScript modules are organized into two tiers:
    20→
    21→**Core/Infrastructure** (3 modules):
    22→- `ui-core.js` — Tab routing, modal management, entry point
    23→- `api-client.js` — HTTP communication layer with retry/error handling
    24→- `state-manager.js` — Session lifecycle, localStorage persistence, state initialization
    25→
    26→**Feature Modules** (11 modules):
    27→- `job-input.js` — Multi-mode job ingestion (text, file, URL)
    28→- `analysis.js` — Job analysis orchestration and post-analysis Q&A
    29→- `analysis-tab.js` — Analysis results rendering
    30→- `customizations.js` — Experience/skill selection and content sorting UI
    31→- `cv-editor.js` — CV data editing interface (master data)
    32→- `cv-generation.js` — Generated CV display and preview
    33→- `rewrites.js` — Rewrite proposal review and decision UI
    34→- `spell-check.js` — Spell/grammar checking UI
    35→- `workflow.js` — Conversation phase tracking and message display
    36→- `utils.js` — Text processing, formatting, truncation utilities
    37→
    38→### D11.2 — Single CSS File with Component Organization
    39→All styles extracted to `styles.css` organized into 31 sections by component (layout, conversation, auth, workflow steps, tabs, forms, tables, cards, analysis display, questions panel, editor, modals, confidence badges, buttons, loading states, etc.). Single CSS file for simplicity (no CSS module tooling).
    40→
    41→### D11.3 — Dependency Graph and Entry Point
    42→- **Entry point**: `ui-core.js` — loads on DOM ready, initializes state-manager, wires up tab routing
    43→- **Tier 1 (infrastructure)**: `state-manager.js`, `api-client.js` — no dependencies within js/
    44→- **Tier 2 (features)**: All feature modules depend on `api-client.js` and/or `state-manager.js`; may depend on `utils.js`
    45→- **No circular dependencies** — workflow.js will depend on other feature modules only for rendering message responses, not vice versa
    46→
    47→### D11.4 — Five-Phase Migration Strategy
    48→1. **Extract utilities** — Move `utils.js` (120 lines) with no dependencies
    49→2. **Extract infrastructure** — Move `api-client.js` and `state-manager.js`; wire into ui-core.js entry point
    50→3. **Extract CSS** — Move all stylesheets to `styles.css`; verify no regressions via browser
    51→4. **Extract feature modules** — Move remaining 11 modules one-by-one, following dependency order
    52→5. **Clean up and test** — Verify all 308 tests pass; run `/cvUiReview`; run `/codeReview`
    53→
    54→### D11.5 — No Breaking Changes to HTML Structure
    55→The single-page app's HTML structure (conversation div, tabs, message containers, editor forms, download section) remains unchanged. Modules interact via:
    56→- Direct DOM manipulation (querySelector, innerHTML, classList)
    57→- Explicit function calls within feature flows
    58→- Message event handlers registered on `window` or global state
    59→- No virtual DOM, no frameworks — vanilla JS patterns preserved
    60→
    61→### D11.6 — Backward Compatibility with Existing Session State
    62→Modules are responsible for reading/writing their respective parts of `window.interactiveState` and localStorage. No changes to session schema or API contracts — migration is 100% backward-compatible.
    63→
    64→---
    65→
    66→## Implementation Tasks
    67→
    68→### Task 11.1: Extract Utility Functions to `web/utils.js` (≈0.5 h)
    69→
    70→**Scope**: Extract all helper functions with no dependencies on DOM or state.
    71→
    72→**Functions to extract** (~120 lines):
    73→- `truncateText(text, maxLength)` — Truncate to N chars with ellipsis
    74→- `formatDate(date)` — Format date/time for display
    75→- `htmlEscape(text)` — HTML entity encoding
    76→- `stripHtml(html)` — Remove HTML tags
    77→- `ordinal(n)` — Convert 1 → "1st", 2 → "2nd", etc.
    78→- `pluralize(count, singular, plural)` — Conditional pluralization
    79→- `capitalizeWords(text)` — Capitalize each word
    80→- Any string comparison, regex, or formatting utility
    81→
    82→**Verification**:
    83→1. Create `web/utils.js` with all utility function definitions
    84→2. Import in `index.html`: `<script src="utils.js"></script>` (before ui-core.js)
    85→3. Run manual smoke test: update one utility call in index.html to verify import works
    86→4. No test failures expected (utilities are pure functions)
    87→
    88→---
    89→
    90→### Task 11.2: Extract API Client to `web/api-client.js` (≈0.5 h)
    91→
    92→**Scope**: All HTTP communication abstracted into a single client module.
    93→
    94→**Functions to extract** (~150 lines):
    95→- `async function apiCall(method, endpoint, data = null)` — Core fetch wrapper with logging
    96→- `async function loadSession(sessionId)` — `GET /api/sessions/{sessionId}`
    97→- `async function fetchStatus()` — `GET /api/status`
    98→- `async function sendAction(phase, action, data = {})` — `POST /api/actions`
    99→- `async function fetchRewrites()` — `GET /api/rewrites`
   100→- `async function approveRewrites(decisions)` — `POST /api/rewrites/approve`
```

```text
## Approved Plan:
# Frontend Refactoring: Modularize web/index.html

## Context

Phase 10 (Persuasion Checks + Loading State) has been completed successfully. However, the monolithic `web/index.html` file has grown to 5,484 lines (862 HTML, 763 CSS, 4,617 JS), making it difficult to:

1. **Maintain and extend** — Finding relevant code requires scrolling through thousands of lines
2. **Test in isolation** — No clear module boundaries for unit testing UI logic
3. **Onboard contributors** — No clear separation of concerns or feature ownership
4. **Debug effectively** — Function dependencies are implicit and scattered throughout

This refactoring breaks `web/index.html` into **15 focused modules** (14 JS + 1 CSS file) organized by responsibility, with a clear entry point (`ui-core.js`) and explicit dependency graph. The refactoring preserves all functionality while enabling future feature development and testing.

---

## Design Decisions

### D11.1 — Module Organization by Responsibility
JavaScript modules are organized into two tiers:

**Core/Infrastructure** (3 modules):
- `ui-core.js` — Tab routing, modal management, entry point
- `api-client.js` — HTTP communication layer with retry/error handling
- `state-manager.js` — Session lifecycle, localStorage persistence, state initialization

**Feature Modules** (11 modules):
- `job-input.js` — Multi-mode job ingestion (text, file, URL)
- `analysis.js` — Job analysis orchestration and post-analysis Q&A
- `analysis-tab.js` — Analysis results rendering
- `customizations.js` — Experience/skill selection and content sorting UI
- `cv-editor.js` — CV data editing interface (master data)
- `cv-generation.js` — Generated CV display and preview
...
</persisted-output>
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→"""
     2→Conversation Manager for LLM-driven CV generation.
     3→
     4→Handles the interactive conversation flow, state management,
     5→and user interaction.
     6→"""
     7→
     8→import json
     9→import re
    10→from datetime import datetime
    11→from pathlib import Path
    12→from typing import Dict, List, Optional
    13→import readline  # Enable line editing and history for input()
    14→
    15→from .llm_client import LLMClient
    16→from .cv_orchestrator import CVOrchestrator
    17→from .config import get_config
    18→
    19→
    20→class ConversationManager:
    21→    """Manages conversational flow for CV generation."""
    22→    
    23→    def __init__(
    24→        self,
    25→        orchestrator: CVOrchestrator,
    26→        llm_client: LLMClient,
    27→        config=None
    28→    ):
    29→        self.orchestrator = orchestrator
    30→        self.llm = llm_client
    31→        self.config = config or get_config()
    32→        self.conversation_history: List[Dict[str, str]] = []
    33→        self.state = {
    34→            'phase':              'init',  # init, job_analysis, customization, rewrite_review, spell_check, generation, refinement
    35→            'position_name':      None,
    36→            'job_description':    None,
    37→            'job_analysis':       None,
    38→            'post_analysis_questions': [],
    39→            'post_analysis_answers':   {},
    40→            'customizations':     None,
    41→            'generated_files':    None,
    42→            'pending_rewrites':   None,   # List[Dict] from propose_rewrites
    43→            'persuasion_warnings': [],    # List[Dict] from run_persuasion_checks (Phase 10)
    44→            'generation_progress': [],    # List[Dict] step-by-step progress from generate_cv (Phase 10)
    45→            'approved_rewrites':  [],     # List[Dict] user-accepted or user-edited
    46→            'rewrite_audit':      [],     # full record: proposal + outcome for metadata
    47→        }
    48→        self.session_dir: Optional[Path] = None
    49→        # Readline history file
    50→        self.history_file: Path = Path(self.config.get('session.history_file', 'files/.input_history'))
    51→    
    52→    def _get_multiline_input(self) -> str:
    53→        """Get multi-line input with terminators (DONE/END) and QUIT.
    54→
    55→        DONE/END: finish multi-line entry
    56→        QUIT: confirm, save session, and exit application
    57→        """
    58→        print(
    59→            "(Enter multiple lines. Type 'DONE' or 'END' on a line by itself; "
    60→            "type 'QUIT' to exit)"
    61→        )
    62→        
    63→        lines = []
    64→        
    65→        while True:
    66→            try:
    67→                line = input()
    68→                
    69→                # Check for terminator keywords
    70→                upper = line.strip().upper()
    71→                if upper in ['DONE', 'END']:
    72→                    break
    73→                elif upper == 'QUIT':
    74→                    confirm = input("\n⚠ Confirm exit? (yes/no): ")
    75→                    if confirm.lower() in ['yes', 'y']:
    76→                        self._save_session()
    77→                        self._save_readline_history()
    78→                        print("\n✓ Session saved. Goodbye!")
    79→                        raise SystemExit(0)
    80→                    else:
    81→                        print("\n✓ Exit cancelled. Continue entering text.")
    82→                        continue
    83→                else:
    84→                    lines.append(line)
    85→                    
    86→            except EOFError:
    87→                # Ctrl+D pressed
    88→                break
    89→        
    90→        return '\n'.join(lines).strip()
    91→    
    92→    def start_interactive(self):
    93→        """Start interactive conversation loop."""
    94→        self._print_welcome()
    95→        # Ensure a position is selected or created
    96→        self._ensure_position_selected()
    97→        self._setup_readline()
```
